title.zh: 卷二十七中之下 五行志
title.en: Volume 27c: Treatise on the Five Elements 3
title.raw: 卷二十七中之下 五行志 Volume 27c: Treatise on the Five Elements 3

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「視之不明，是謂不悊，厥咎舒，厥罰恆奧，厥極疾。

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "When sight is not clear, this is called lacking discernment; its fault is laxity, its punishment is constant heat, and its extreme is illness.
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "When sight is not clear, this is called lacking discernment; its fault is laxity, its punishment is constant heat, and its extreme is illness.

sentences.zh: 時則有草妖，時則有蠃蟲之孽，時則有羊禍，時則有目痾，時則有赤眚赤祥。

translations.literal: Then at times there are plant anomalies, at times prodigies of shell creatures, at times sheep calamities, at times eye afflictions, and at times red blights and red portents.
translations.idiomatic: Then at times there are plant anomalies, at times prodigies of shell creatures, at times sheep calamities, at times eye afflictions, and at times red blights and red portents.

sentences.zh: 惟水沴火。」

translations.literal: Only Water afflicts Fire."
translations.idiomatic: Only Water afflicts Fire."

sentences.zh: 「視之不明，是謂不悊」，悊，知也。

translations.literal: "Sight not clear, this is called lacking discernment." Zhe means knowledge.
translations.idiomatic: "Sight not clear, this is called lacking discernment." Zhe means knowledge.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：「爾德不明，以亡陪亡卿；

translations.literal: The Odes says: "Your virtue is not clear, and so no assistant and no high minister remain;
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: "Your virtue is not clear, and so no assistant and no high minister remain;

sentences.zh: 不明爾德，以亡背亡仄。」

translations.literal: not making your virtue clear, and so no backing and no support remain."
translations.idiomatic: not making your virtue clear, and so no backing and no support remain."

sentences.zh: 言上不明，暗昧蔽惑，則不能知善惡，親近習，長同類，亡功者受賞，有罪者不殺，百官廢亂，失在舒緩，故其咎舒也。

translations.literal: It means that if those above are not clear, dark and deluded, they cannot distinguish good and evil, they draw close to familiars and foster their own kind; those without merit receive reward, those with guilt are not executed, and the hundred offices fall into disorder. The failure lies in slackness, therefore its blame is laxity.
translations.idiomatic: It means that if those above are not clear, dark and deluded, they cannot distinguish good and evil, they draw close to familiars and foster their own kind; those without merit receive reward, those with guilt are not executed, and the hundred offices fall into disorder. The failure lies in slackness, therefore its blame is laxity.

sentences.zh: 盛夏日長，暑以養物，政弛緩，故其罰常奧也。

translations.literal: In high summer days are long and heat nourishes things; when governance is loose, therefore the punishment is persistent heat.
translations.idiomatic: In high summer days are long and heat nourishes things; when governance is loose, therefore the punishment is persistent heat.

sentences.zh: 奧則冬溫，春夏不和，傷病民人，故極疾也。

translations.literal: If heat persists, winter is warm and spring-summer are out of harmony, harming and sickening the people; therefore the extreme is illness.
translations.idiomatic: If heat persists, winter is warm and spring-summer are out of harmony, harming and sickening the people; therefore the extreme is illness.

sentences.zh: 誅不行則霜不殺草，繇臣下則殺不以時，故有草妖。

translations.literal: If punishments are not carried out, frost does not kill grass. From ministers below, executions are not timely; therefore there are plant anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: If punishments are not carried out, frost does not kill grass. From ministers below, executions are not timely; therefore there are plant anomalies.

sentences.zh: 凡妖，貌則以服，言則以詩，聽則以聲。

translations.literal: In general, anomalies: for bearing they appear in clothing; for speech, in songs; for hearing, in sounds.
translations.idiomatic: In general, anomalies: for bearing they appear in clothing; for speech, in songs; for hearing, in sounds.

sentences.zh: 視則以色者，五色物之大分也，在於眚祥，故聖人以為草妖，失秉之明者也。

translations.literal: For sight they appear in colors. Since color is the great division of the five colors and appears in blights and portents, sages considered plant anomalies to indicate lost clarity in holding authority.
translations.idiomatic: For sight they appear in colors. Since color is the great division of the five colors and appears in blights and portents, sages considered plant anomalies to indicate lost clarity in holding authority.

sentences.zh: 溫奧生蟲，故有蠃蟲之孽，謂螟螣之類當死不死，未當生而生，或多於故而為災也。

translations.literal: Warm heat breeds insects; therefore there are prodigies of shell-creatures, meaning borers and locust-like kinds that should die but do not, or are born when not due, or become excessive and cause disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Warm heat breeds insects; therefore there are prodigies of shell-creatures, meaning borers and locust-like kinds that should die but do not, or are born when not due, or become excessive and cause disaster.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為屬思心不容。

translations.literal: Liu Xin took this as belonging to thought and heart not contained.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin took this as belonging to thought and heart not contained.

sentences.zh: 於易，剛而包柔為離，離為火為目。

translations.literal: In the Changes, firm enclosing soft is Li; Li is fire and is the eye.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, firm enclosing soft is Li; Li is fire and is the eye.

sentences.zh: 羊上角下號，剛而包柔，羊大目而不精明，視氣毀故有羊禍。

translations.literal: Sheep have horns above and openings below, firm enclosing soft; sheep have large eyes but not keen sight. Since sight-qi is damaged, therefore there are sheep calamities.
translations.idiomatic: Sheep have horns above and openings below, firm enclosing soft; sheep have large eyes but not keen sight. Since sight-qi is damaged, therefore there are sheep calamities.

sentences.zh: 一曰，暑歲羊多疫死，及為怪，亦是也。

translations.literal: Another view says: in hot years many sheep die of epidemic or become strange; this also belongs here.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says: in hot years many sheep die of epidemic or become strange; this also belongs here.

sentences.zh: 及人，則多病目者，故有目痾。

translations.literal: For people, there are many eye diseases; therefore there are eye afflictions.
translations.idiomatic: For people, there are many eye diseases; therefore there are eye afflictions.

sentences.zh: 火色赤，故有赤眚赤祥。

translations.literal: Fire's color is red; therefore there are red blights and red portents.
translations.idiomatic: Fire's color is red; therefore there are red blights and red portents.

sentences.zh: 凡視傷者病火氣，火氣傷則水沴之。

translations.literal: Wherever sight is harmed, fire-qi is diseased; when fire-qi is diseased, water afflicts it.
translations.idiomatic: Wherever sight is harmed, fire-qi is diseased; when fire-qi is diseased, water afflicts it.

sentences.zh: 其極疾者，順之，其福曰壽。

translations.literal: Its extreme is illness; if one follows the proper course, its blessing is longevity.
translations.idiomatic: Its extreme is illness; if one follows the proper course, its blessing is longevity.

sentences.zh: 劉歆視傳曰有羽蟲之孽，雞禍。

translations.literal: Liu Xin's transmission on sight says there are prodigies of feathered creatures and fowl calamities.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin's transmission on sight says there are prodigies of feathered creatures and fowl calamities.

sentences.zh: 說以為於天文南方喙為鳥星，故為羽蟲；

translations.literal: Its explanation says that in astronomy, Beak in the south is a bird star, therefore feathered creatures;
translations.idiomatic: Its explanation says that in astronomy, Beak in the south is a bird star, therefore feathered creatures;

sentences.zh: 禍亦從羽，故為雞；

translations.literal: the calamity likewise follows feathered kinds, therefore fowl;
translations.idiomatic: the calamity likewise follows feathered kinds, therefore fowl;

sentences.zh: 雞於易自在巽。

translations.literal: in the Changes, fowl belongs to Xun.
translations.idiomatic: in the Changes, fowl belongs to Xun.

sentences.zh: 說非是。

translations.literal: This explanation is incorrect.
translations.idiomatic: This explanation is incorrect.

sentences.zh: 庶徵之恆奧，劉向以為春秋亡冰也。

translations.literal: For the regular sign of persistent heat, Liu Xiang took it as winters without ice in the Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: For the regular sign of persistent heat, Liu Xiang took it as winters without ice in the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 小奧不書，無冰然後書，舉其大者也。

translations.literal: Minor heat was not recorded; only no-ice was recorded, taking the major case.
translations.idiomatic: Minor heat was not recorded; only no-ice was recorded, taking the major case.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「祿不遂行茲謂欺，厥咎奧，雨雪四至而溫。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When emoluments are not properly carried out, this is called deceit; its blame is heat, with rain and snow arriving in all four seasons yet still warm.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When emoluments are not properly carried out, this is called deceit; its blame is heat, with rain and snow arriving in all four seasons yet still warm.

sentences.zh: 臣安祿樂逸茲謂亂，奧而生蟲。

translations.literal: When ministers rest secure in salary and delight in ease, this is called disorder; heat then breeds insects.
translations.idiomatic: When ministers rest secure in salary and delight in ease, this is called disorder; heat then breeds insects.

sentences.zh: 知罪不誅茲謂舒，其奧，夏則暑殺人，冬則物華實。

translations.literal: When one knows guilt but does not punish, this is called laxity; in such heat, summer brings killing heat, and winter brings flowering and fruiting.
translations.idiomatic: When one knows guilt but does not punish, this is called laxity; in such heat, summer brings killing heat, and winter brings flowering and fruiting.

sentences.zh: 重過不誅，茲謂亡徵，其咎當寒而奧六日也。」

translations.literal: If repeated offenses are not punished, this is called lack of verification; its blame is that when it should be cold, there is heat for six days."
translations.idiomatic: If repeated offenses are not punished, this is called lack of verification; its blame is that when it should be cold, there is heat for six days."

sentences.zh: 桓公十五年「春，亡冰」。

translations.literal: In Duke Huan year fifteen: "In spring, no ice."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Huan year fifteen: "In spring, no ice."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為周春，今冬也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that Zhou spring is now winter.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that Zhou spring is now winter.

sentences.zh: 先是連兵鄰國，三戰而再敗也，內失百姓，外失諸侯，不敢行誅罰，鄭伯突篡兄而立，公與相親，長養同類，不明善惡之罰也。

translations.literal: Before this there had been continuous warfare with neighboring states, three campaigns with two defeats; internally he lost the people, externally he lost the feudal lords, and did not dare enforce punishments. Earl Tu of Zheng usurped his elder brother and took the throne; the duke was intimate with him, fostering his own kind. This was punishment for failing to distinguish good and evil.
translations.idiomatic: Before this there had been continuous warfare with neighboring states, three campaigns with two defeats; internally he lost the people, externally he lost the feudal lords, and did not dare enforce punishments. Earl Tu of Zheng usurped his elder brother and took the throne; the duke was intimate with him, fostering his own kind. This was punishment for failing to distinguish good and evil.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為象夫人不正，陰失節也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that it symbolized the consort being improper and yin losing its proper rhythm.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that it symbolized the consort being improper and yin losing its proper rhythm.

sentences.zh: 成公元年「二月，無冰」。

translations.literal: In Duke Cheng year one: "In the second month, no ice."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng year one: "In the second month, no ice."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為方有宣公之喪，君臣無悲哀之心，而炕陽，作丘甲。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that they were in mourning for Duke Xuan, yet ruler and ministers had no grieving heart, instead exalting yang and creating mound-armor levies.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that they were in mourning for Duke Xuan, yet ruler and ministers had no grieving heart, instead exalting yang and creating mound-armor levies.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為時公幼弱，政舒緩也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that the duke was young and weak and governance was slack.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that the duke was young and weak and governance was slack.

sentences.zh: 襄公二十八年「春，無冰」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xiang year twenty-eight: "In spring, no ice."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang year twenty-eight: "In spring, no ice."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是公作三軍，有侵陵用武之意，於是鄰國不和，伐其三鄙，被兵十有餘年，因之以饑饉，百姓怨望，臣下心離，公懼而弛緩，不敢行誅罰，楚有夷狄行，公有從楚心，不明善惡之應。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this the duke created three armies and had intent to invade and use force; thus neighboring states were disharmonious and attacked his three border districts. Warfare lasted over ten years, followed by famine; the people resented him, ministers' hearts parted, and the duke became fearful and lax, not daring to carry out punishments. Chu practiced barbarian ways, and the duke had a mind to follow Chu. This was the response to not distinguishing good and evil.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this the duke created three armies and had intent to invade and use force; thus neighboring states were disharmonious and attacked his three border districts. Warfare lasted over ten years, followed by famine; the people resented him, ministers' hearts parted, and the duke became fearful and lax, not daring to carry out punishments. Chu practiced barbarian ways, and the duke had a mind to follow Chu. This was the response to not distinguishing good and evil.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒指略同。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.

sentences.zh: 一曰，水旱之災，寒暑之變，天下皆同，故曰「無冰」，天下異也。

translations.literal: Another view says disasters of flood-drought and shifts of cold-heat are the same across the realm; therefore "no ice" means an anomaly throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says disasters of flood-drought and shifts of cold-heat are the same across the realm; therefore "no ice" means an anomaly throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 桓公殺兄弒君，外成宋亂，與鄭易邑，背畔周室。

translations.literal: Duke Huan killed his elder brother and regicide-lord, externally fostering Song's disorder, exchanging towns with Zheng, and betraying the Zhou house.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan killed his elder brother and regicide-lord, externally fostering Song's disorder, exchanging towns with Zheng, and betraying the Zhou house.

sentences.zh: 成公時，楚橫行中國，王札子殺召伯、毛伯，晉敗天子之師于貿戎，天子皆不能討。

translations.literal: In Duke Cheng's time, Chu ran rampant in the central states, Prince Zha murdered Duke Zhao and Duke Mao, and Jin defeated the Son of Heaven's army at Mao-Rong; the Son of Heaven could not punish them.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng's time, Chu ran rampant in the central states, Prince Zha murdered Duke Zhao and Duke Mao, and Jin defeated the Son of Heaven's army at Mao-Rong; the Son of Heaven could not punish them.

sentences.zh: 襄公時，天下諸侯之大夫皆執國權，君不能制。

translations.literal: In Duke Xiang's time, great officers of all feudal states held state power, and rulers could not control them.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang's time, great officers of all feudal states held state power, and rulers could not control them.

sentences.zh: 漸將日甚，善惡不明，誅罰不行。

translations.literal: This gradually worsened day by day: good and evil were unclear, punishments unenforced.
translations.idiomatic: This gradually worsened day by day: good and evil were unclear, punishments unenforced.

sentences.zh: 周失之舒，秦失之急，故周衰亡寒歲，秦滅亡奧年。

translations.literal: Zhou erred in laxity, Qin erred in haste; therefore Zhou declined in cold years and Qin was destroyed in hot years.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou erred in laxity, Qin erred in haste; therefore Zhou declined in cold years and Qin was destroyed in hot years.

sentences.zh: 武帝元狩六年冬，亡冰。

translations.literal: In winter of Emperor Wu's Yuanshou year six, there was no ice.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of Emperor Wu's Yuanshou year six, there was no ice.

sentences.zh: 先是，比年遣大將軍衛青、霍去病攻祁連，絕大幕，窮追單于，斬首十餘萬級，還，大行慶賞。

translations.literal: Before this, for years the Great General Wei Qing and Huo Qubing were sent to strike Qilian, cross the desert, pursue the Chanyu to exhaustion, and behead over one hundred thousand. On return, great celebrations and rewards were held.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, for years the Great General Wei Qing and Huo Qubing were sent to strike Qilian, cross the desert, pursue the Chanyu to exhaustion, and behead over one hundred thousand. On return, great celebrations and rewards were held.

sentences.zh: 乃閔海內勤勞，是歲遣博士褚大等六人持節巡行天下，存賜鰥寡，假與乏困，舉遺逸獨行君子詣行在所。

translations.literal: Then, pitying the hardship of all under heaven, that year six erudites including Chu Da were sent with tallies to inspect the realm, comfort widows and widowers, lend to the needy, and recommend recluses and solitary gentlemen to the imperial traveling court.
translations.idiomatic: Then, pitying the hardship of all under heaven, that year six erudites including Chu Da were sent with tallies to inspect the realm, comfort widows and widowers, lend to the needy, and recommend recluses and solitary gentlemen to the imperial traveling court.

sentences.zh: 郡國有以為便宜者，上丞相、御史以聞。

translations.literal: If commanderies and kingdoms had policy conveniences, they submitted them to Chancellor and Censor to report upward.
translations.idiomatic: If commanderies and kingdoms had policy conveniences, they submitted them to Chancellor and Censor to report upward.

sentences.zh: 天下咸喜。

translations.literal: All under heaven rejoiced.
translations.idiomatic: All under heaven rejoiced.

sentences.zh: 昭帝始元二年冬，亡冰。

translations.literal: In winter of Shiyuan year two under Emperor Zhao, there was no ice.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of Shiyuan year two under Emperor Zhao, there was no ice.

sentences.zh: 是時上年九歲，大將軍霍光秉政，始行寬緩，欲以說下。

translations.literal: At that time the ruler was nine years old; Grand General Huo Guang held power and first implemented lenient, eased governance to please those below.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the ruler was nine years old; Grand General Huo Guang held power and first implemented lenient, eased governance to please those below.

sentences.zh: 僖公三十三年「十二月，隕霜不殺草」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year thirty-three: "In the twelfth month, falling frost did not kill grass."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year thirty-three: "In the twelfth month, falling frost did not kill grass."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為草妖也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin took this as a plant anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin took this as a plant anomaly.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為今十月，周十二月。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held it is now tenth month, corresponding to Zhou twelfth month.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held it is now tenth month, corresponding to Zhou twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 於易，五為天位，為君位，九月陰氣至，五通於天位，其卦為剝，剝落萬物，始大殺矣，明陰從陽命，臣受君令而後殺也。

translations.literal: In the Changes, five is the Heavenly position and ruler position. By ninth month yin qi arrives; five connects to Heaven-position, its hexagram is Stripping, stripping away all things and beginning great killing. This shows yin following yang command, ministers receiving ruler command and only then killing.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, five is the Heavenly position and ruler position. By ninth month yin qi arrives; five connects to Heaven-position, its hexagram is Stripping, stripping away all things and beginning great killing. This shows yin following yang command, ministers receiving ruler command and only then killing.

sentences.zh: 今十月隕霜而不能殺草，此君誅不行，舒緩之應也。

translations.literal: Now in tenth month frost fell yet could not kill grass: this was response to the ruler's punishments not being enforced and governance being lax.
translations.idiomatic: Now in tenth month frost fell yet could not kill grass: this was response to the ruler's punishments not being enforced and governance being lax.

sentences.zh: 是時公子遂顓權，三桓始世官，天戒若曰，自此之後，將皆為亂矣。

translations.literal: At this time Prince Sui monopolized power and the Three Huan began hereditary office. Heaven's warning was as if saying: from now on, all will become disorder.
translations.idiomatic: At this time Prince Sui monopolized power and the Three Huan began hereditary office. Heaven's warning was as if saying: from now on, all will become disorder.

sentences.zh: 文公不寤，其後遂殺子赤，三家逐昭公。

translations.literal: Duke Wen did not awaken; later there was the killing of Zichi, and the Three Families expelled Duke Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Wen did not awaken; later there was the killing of Zichi, and the Three Families expelled Duke Zhao.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒指略同。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「臣有緩茲謂不順，厥異霜不殺也。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers are slow, this is called noncompliance; its anomaly is frost that does not kill."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers are slow, this is called noncompliance; its anomaly is frost that does not kill."

sentences.zh: 書序曰：「伊涉相太戊，亳有祥桑穀共生。」

translations.literal: The Book preface says: "When Yi Zhi assisted Taijia, at Bo there was the portent of mulberry and grain growing together."
translations.idiomatic: The Book preface says: "When Yi Zhi assisted Taijia, at Bo there was the portent of mulberry and grain growing together."

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「俱生乎朝，七日而大拱。

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "They grew together in the court and in seven days became great enough to encircle with arms.
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "They grew together in the court and in seven days became great enough to encircle with arms.

sentences.zh: 伊陟戒以修德，而木枯。」

translations.literal: Yi Zhi admonished with self-cultivation of virtue, and the trees withered."
translations.idiomatic: Yi Zhi admonished with self-cultivation of virtue, and the trees withered."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為殷道既衰，高宗承敝而起，盡涼陰之哀，天下應之，既獲顯榮，怠於政事，國將危亡，故桑穀之異見。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that after Shang's way had declined, Gaozong rose amid decay, fully bearing the grief of cool yin and winning the realm's response. Once he gained manifest glory, he grew negligent in state affairs and the state neared danger and ruin; therefore the anomaly of mulberry and grain appeared.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that after Shang's way had declined, Gaozong rose amid decay, fully bearing the grief of cool yin and winning the realm's response. Once he gained manifest glory, he grew negligent in state affairs and the state neared danger and ruin; therefore the anomaly of mulberry and grain appeared.

sentences.zh: 桑猶喪也，穀猶生也，殺生之秉失而在下，近草妖也。

translations.literal: Mulberry sounds like mourning; grain sounds like growth. The authority over killing and giving life was misplaced below; this is near a plant anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: Mulberry sounds like mourning; grain sounds like growth. The authority over killing and giving life was misplaced below; this is near a plant anomaly.

sentences.zh: 一曰，野木生朝而暴長，小人將暴在大臣之位，危亡國家，象朝將為虛之應也。

translations.literal: Another view: wild trees growing in court and suddenly growing tall means petty men will suddenly occupy great-minister positions, endangering and ruining the state; it portends the court becoming empty.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: wild trees growing in court and suddenly growing tall means petty men will suddenly occupy great-minister positions, endangering and ruining the state; it portends the court becoming empty.

sentences.zh: 書序又曰：「高宗祭成湯，有蜚雉登鼎耳而雊。」

translations.literal: The Book preface also says: "When Gaozong sacrificed to Cheng Tang, a pheasant flew to the handle of the tripod and crowed."
translations.idiomatic: The Book preface also says: "When Gaozong sacrificed to Cheng Tang, a pheasant flew to the handle of the tripod and crowed."

sentences.zh: 祖己曰：「惟先假王，正厥事。」

translations.literal: Zu Ji said: "Only first borrow the king's authority and rectify his affairs."
translations.idiomatic: Zu Ji said: "Only first borrow the king's authority and rectify his affairs."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為雉雊鳴者雄也，以赤色為主。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that the crowing pheasant was male and chiefly red in color.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that the crowing pheasant was male and chiefly red in color.

sentences.zh: 於易，離為雉，雉，南方，近赤祥也。

translations.literal: In the Changes, Li is pheasant; pheasant is the south, near red portent.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, Li is pheasant; pheasant is the south, near red portent.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為羽蟲之孽。

translations.literal: Liu Xin took it as a prodigy of feathered creatures.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin took it as a prodigy of feathered creatures.

sentences.zh: 易有鼎卦，鼎，宗廟之器，主器奉宗廟者長子也。

translations.literal: The Changes has the Ding hexagram. The tripod is a vessel of the ancestral temple; the one in charge of vessels and serving the temple is the eldest son.
translations.idiomatic: The Changes has the Ding hexagram. The tripod is a vessel of the ancestral temple; the one in charge of vessels and serving the temple is the eldest son.

sentences.zh: 野鳥自外來，入為宗廟器主，是繼嗣將易也。

translations.literal: A wild bird coming from outside and entering to become lord of temple vessels means succession will change.
translations.idiomatic: A wild bird coming from outside and entering to become lord of temple vessels means succession will change.

sentences.zh: 一曰，鼎三足，三公象，而以耳行。

translations.literal: Another view: the tripod has three legs, image of the Three Dukes, and functions through its handles.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: the tripod has three legs, image of the Three Dukes, and functions through its handles.

sentences.zh: 野鳥居鼎耳，小人將居公位，敗宗廟之祀。

translations.literal: A wild bird perching on the handle means petty men will occupy ducal rank and damage temple sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: A wild bird perching on the handle means petty men will occupy ducal rank and damage temple sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 野木生朝，野鳥入廟，敗亡之異也。

translations.literal: Wild trees growing in court and wild birds entering the temple are anomalies of defeat and ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Wild trees growing in court and wild birds entering the temple are anomalies of defeat and ruin.

sentences.zh: 武丁恐駭，謀於忠賢，修德而正事，內舉傅說，授以國政，外伐鬼方，以安諸夏，故能攘木鳥之妖，致百年之壽，所謂「六沴作見，若是共御，五福乃降，用章于下」者也。

translations.literal: Wuding was alarmed and consulted loyal worthies; he cultivated virtue and rectified affairs, internally promoted Fu Yue and entrusted him with state government, externally campaigned against Guifang to pacify the Xia states. Thus he repelled the anomalies of trees and birds and attained a hundred-year lifespan, what is meant by: "When the six noxious forces appear, if they are jointly controlled, the five blessings then descend and become manifest below."
translations.idiomatic: Wuding was alarmed and consulted loyal worthies; he cultivated virtue and rectified affairs, internally promoted Fu Yue and entrusted him with state government, externally campaigned against Guifang to pacify the Xia states. Thus he repelled the anomalies of trees and birds and attained a hundred-year lifespan, what is meant by: "When the six noxious forces appear, if they are jointly controlled, the five blessings then descend and become manifest below."

sentences.zh: 一曰，金沴木曰木不曲直。

translations.literal: Another view says: metal afflicting wood means wood not bending and straightening.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says: metal afflicting wood means wood not bending and straightening.

sentences.zh: 僖公三十三年「十二月，李梅實」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year thirty-three: "In the twelfth month, plums and mume fruited."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year thirty-three: "In the twelfth month, plums and mume fruited."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為周十二月，今十月也，李梅當剝落，今反華實，近草妖也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that Zhou twelfth month is now tenth month; plums and mume should be shedding, yet instead they flowered and fruited, near a plant anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that Zhou twelfth month is now tenth month; plums and mume should be shedding, yet instead they flowered and fruited, near a plant anomaly.

sentences.zh: 先華而後實，不書華，舉重者也。

translations.literal: Flowering first then fruiting: the flowering was not recorded, taking the graver sign.
translations.idiomatic: Flowering first then fruiting: the flowering was not recorded, taking the graver sign.

sentences.zh: 陰成陽事，象臣顓君作威福。

translations.literal: Yin accomplishing yang affairs symbolizes ministers monopolizing rulerly powers of punishment and reward.
translations.idiomatic: Yin accomplishing yang affairs symbolizes ministers monopolizing rulerly powers of punishment and reward.

sentences.zh: 一曰，冬當殺，反生，象驕臣當誅，不行其罰也。

translations.literal: Another view: in winter what should be killed instead grows, symbolizing arrogant ministers who should be punished but are not.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: in winter what should be killed instead grows, symbolizing arrogant ministers who should be punished but are not.

sentences.zh: 故冬華華者，象臣邪謀有端而不成，至於實，則成矣。

translations.literal: Thus winter flowering symbolizes ministers' crooked schemes having beginnings but not completion; reaching fruit means completion.
translations.idiomatic: Thus winter flowering symbolizes ministers' crooked schemes having beginnings but not completion; reaching fruit means completion.

sentences.zh: 是時僖公死，公子遂顓權，文公不寤，後有子赤之變。

translations.literal: At this time Duke Xi died and Prince Sui monopolized power; Duke Wen did not awaken, and later there was the Zichi incident.
translations.idiomatic: At this time Duke Xi died and Prince Sui monopolized power; Duke Wen did not awaken, and later there was the Zichi incident.

sentences.zh: 一曰，君舒緩甚，奧氣不臧，則華實復生。

translations.literal: Another view: when rulerly laxity is extreme and heat-qi is not properly restrained, flowering and fruiting reappear.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: when rulerly laxity is extreme and heat-qi is not properly restrained, flowering and fruiting reappear.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為李梅實，臣下彊也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that plums and mume fruiting meant ministers below were strong.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that plums and mume fruiting meant ministers below were strong.

sentences.zh: 記曰：「不當華而華，易大夫；

translations.literal: The Record says: "Flowering when it should not flower means replacing great officers;
translations.idiomatic: The Record says: "Flowering when it should not flower means replacing great officers;

sentences.zh: 不當實而實，易相室。」

translations.literal: fruiting when it should not fruit means replacing those who assist the ruling house."
translations.idiomatic: fruiting when it should not fruit means replacing those who assist the ruling house."

sentences.zh: 冬，水王，木相，故象大臣。

translations.literal: In winter, Water is king and Wood is minister; therefore it symbolizes great ministers.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, Water is king and Wood is minister; therefore it symbolizes great ministers.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為庶徵皆以蟲為孽，思心蠃蟲孽也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that among the common signs, anomalies are all classed by insects; for thought-heart, shell-creature prodigy.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that among the common signs, anomalies are all classed by insects; for thought-heart, shell-creature prodigy.

sentences.zh: 李梅實，屬草妖。

translations.literal: Plum and mume fruiting belongs to plant anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: Plum and mume fruiting belongs to plant anomaly.

sentences.zh: 惠帝五年十月，桃李華，棗實。

translations.literal: In tenth month of Emperor Hui year five, peaches and plums flowered, and jujubes fruited.
translations.idiomatic: In tenth month of Emperor Hui year five, peaches and plums flowered, and jujubes fruited.

sentences.zh: 昭帝時，上林苑中大柳樹斷仆地，一朝起立，生枝葉，有蟲食其葉，成文字，曰「公孫病已立」。

translations.literal: In Emperor Zhao's time, in Shanglin Park a great willow broke and fell to the ground, then one morning stood up, sprouted branches and leaves, and insects ate its leaves into written characters saying: "Gongsun Bingyi will be established."
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Zhao's time, in Shanglin Park a great willow broke and fell to the ground, then one morning stood up, sprouted branches and leaves, and insects ate its leaves into written characters saying: "Gongsun Bingyi will be established."

sentences.zh: 又昌邑王國社有枯樹復生枝葉。

translations.literal: Also in the state altar of King of Changyi, a withered tree regrew branches and leaves.
translations.idiomatic: Also in the state altar of King of Changyi, a withered tree regrew branches and leaves.

sentences.zh: 眭孟以為木陰類，下民象，當有故廢之家公孫氏從民間受命為天子者。

translations.literal: Sui Meng held that wood belongs to yin and symbolizes common people; there should be one from a formerly deposed house, of the Gongsun clan, receiving mandate from among the people to become Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Sui Meng held that wood belongs to yin and symbolizes common people; there should be one from a formerly deposed house, of the Gongsun clan, receiving mandate from among the people to become Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 昭帝富於春秋，霍光秉政，以孟妖言，誅之。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhao was still young, and Huo Guang held power; because of Meng's ominous speech, he had him executed.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhao was still young, and Huo Guang held power; because of Meng's ominous speech, he had him executed.

sentences.zh: 後昭帝崩，無子，徵昌邑王賀嗣位，狂亂失道，光廢之，更立昭帝兄衛太子之孫，是為宣帝。

translations.literal: Later Emperor Zhao died without sons. King He of Changyi was summoned to succeed, but he was deranged and lawless. Huo Guang deposed him and installed the grandson of Emperor Zhao's elder brother, the Wei Crown Prince; this was Emperor Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Later Emperor Zhao died without sons. King He of Changyi was summoned to succeed, but he was deranged and lawless. Huo Guang deposed him and installed the grandson of Emperor Zhao's elder brother, the Wei Crown Prince; this was Emperor Xuan.

sentences.zh: 帝本名病已。

translations.literal: The emperor's original name was Bingyi.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's original name was Bingyi.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「枯楊生稊，枯木復生，人君亡子。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "A withered poplar sprouting shoots, dead wood reviving: the ruler has no son."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "A withered poplar sprouting shoots, dead wood reviving: the ruler has no son."

sentences.zh: 元帝初元四年，皇后曾祖父濟南東平陵王伯墓門梓柱卒生枝葉，上出屋。

translations.literal: In Chuyuan year four under Emperor Yuan, at the tomb gate of Wang Bo, great-grandfather of the empress at Dongpingling in Jinan, a catalpa pillar suddenly grew branches and leaves reaching above the roof.
translations.idiomatic: In Chuyuan year four under Emperor Yuan, at the tomb gate of Wang Bo, great-grandfather of the empress at Dongpingling in Jinan, a catalpa pillar suddenly grew branches and leaves reaching above the roof.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為王氏貴盛將代漢家之象也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took this as a sign that the Wang clan's noble power would replace the Han house.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a sign that the Wang clan's noble power would replace the Han house.

sentences.zh: 後王莽篡位，自說之曰：「初元四年，莽生之歲也，當漢九世火德之厄，而有此祥興於高祖考之門。

translations.literal: After Wang Mang usurped, he explained it himself: "Chuyuan year four was the year I was born. It coincided with the calamity of Han's ninth generation of fire virtue, and this portent arose at my high ancestor's gate.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Mang usurped, he explained it himself: "Chuyuan year four was the year I was born. It coincided with the calamity of Han's ninth generation of fire virtue, and this portent arose at my high ancestor's gate.

sentences.zh: 門為開通，梓猶子也，言王氏當有賢子開通祖統，起於柱石大臣之位，受命而王之符也。」

translations.literal: The gate means opening and passage; zi (catalpa) sounds like zi (son). It says the Wang clan would have a worthy son opening and continuing ancestral line, rising from the rank of pillar-ministers, receiving mandate and kingship."
translations.idiomatic: The gate means opening and passage; zi (catalpa) sounds like zi (son). It says the Wang clan would have a worthy son opening and continuing ancestral line, rising from the rank of pillar-ministers, receiving mandate and kingship."

sentences.zh: 建昭五年，兗州刺史浩賞禁民私所自立社。

translations.literal: In Jianzhao year five, Hao Shang, Inspector of Yanzhou, forbade people from privately setting up their own shrines.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianzhao year five, Hao Shang, Inspector of Yanzhou, forbade people from privately setting up their own shrines.

sentences.zh: 山陽橐茅鄉社有大槐樹，吏伐斷之，其夜樹復立其故處。

translations.literal: At the shrine of Tuomao township in Shanyang there was a great locust tree; officials cut it down, but that night the tree stood up again in its former place.
translations.idiomatic: At the shrine of Tuomao township in Shanyang there was a great locust tree; officials cut it down, but that night the tree stood up again in its former place.

sentences.zh: 成帝永始元年二月，河南街郵樗樹生支如人頭，眉目須皆具，亡髮耳。

translations.literal: In second month of Yongshi year one under Emperor Cheng, on a tree by a street post in Henan there grew a branch like a human head, complete with eyebrows, eyes, and beard, but without hair or ears.
translations.idiomatic: In second month of Yongshi year one under Emperor Cheng, on a tree by a street post in Henan there grew a branch like a human head, complete with eyebrows, eyes, and beard, but without hair or ears.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平三年十月，汝南西平遂陽鄉柱仆地，生支如人形，身青黃色，面白，頭有务髮，稍長大，凡長六寸一分。

translations.literal: In tenth month of Jianping year three under Emperor Ai, in Suiyang township, Xiping, Runan, a pillar fell to the ground and grew a branch shaped like a human figure: body blue-yellow, face white, with thin hair on the head, gradually growing, total length six cun one fen.
translations.idiomatic: In tenth month of Jianping year three under Emperor Ai, in Suiyang township, Xiping, Runan, a pillar fell to the ground and grew a branch shaped like a human figure: body blue-yellow, face white, with thin hair on the head, gradually growing, total length six cun one fen.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「王德衰，下人將起，則有木生為人狀。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When royal virtue declines and commoners are about to rise, then wood grows in human form."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When royal virtue declines and commoners are about to rise, then wood grows in human form."

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平三年，零陵有樹僵地，圍丈六尺，長十丈七尺。

translations.literal: In Jianping year three under Emperor Ai, in Lingling there was a tree lying stiff on the ground, circumference one zhang six chi, length ten zhang seven chi.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianping year three under Emperor Ai, in Lingling there was a tree lying stiff on the ground, circumference one zhang six chi, length ten zhang seven chi.

sentences.zh: 民斷其本，長九尺餘，皆枯。

translations.literal: People cut its base, leaving over nine chi; all was withered.
translations.idiomatic: People cut its base, leaving over nine chi; all was withered.

sentences.zh: 三月，樹卒自立故處。

translations.literal: In the third month, the tree suddenly stood itself back in its former place.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month, the tree suddenly stood itself back in its former place.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「棄正作淫，厥妖木斷自屬。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Abandoning the proper and practicing licentiousness: its portent is severed wood rejoining itself.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Abandoning the proper and practicing licentiousness: its portent is severed wood rejoining itself.

sentences.zh: 妃后有顓，木仆反立，斷枯復生。

translations.literal: When consorts and empresses monopolize power, felled wood stands again, cut and withered wood revives.
translations.idiomatic: When consorts and empresses monopolize power, felled wood stands again, cut and withered wood revives.

sentences.zh: 天辟惡之。」

translations.literal: Heaven hates this."
translations.idiomatic: Heaven hates this."

sentences.zh: 元帝永光二年八月，天雨草，而葉相摎結，大如彈丸。

translations.literal: In eighth month of Yongguang year two under Emperor Yuan, grass rained from heaven; the leaves were twisted and knotted together, as large as pellets.
translations.idiomatic: In eighth month of Yongguang year two under Emperor Yuan, grass rained from heaven; the leaves were twisted and knotted together, as large as pellets.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始三年正月，天雨草，狀如永光時。

translations.literal: In first month of Yuanshi year three under Emperor Ping, grass rained from heaven, similar in form to Yongguang.
translations.idiomatic: In first month of Yuanshi year three under Emperor Ping, grass rained from heaven, similar in form to Yongguang.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「君吝於祿，信衰賢去，厥妖天雨草。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the ruler is stingy with emoluments, trust decays and worthies depart, its portent is grass raining from heaven."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the ruler is stingy with emoluments, trust decays and worthies depart, its portent is grass raining from heaven."

sentences.zh: 昭公二十五年「夏，有鴝鵒來巢」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhao year twenty-five: "In summer, mynas came to nest."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year twenty-five: "In summer, mynas came to nest."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為羽蟲之孽，其色黑，又黑祥也，視不明聽不聰之罰也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin took it as a prodigy of feathered creatures; its color was black, also a black portent, punishment for sight not clear and hearing not acute.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin took it as a prodigy of feathered creatures; its color was black, also a black portent, punishment for sight not clear and hearing not acute.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為有蜚有蛊不言來者，氣所生，所謂眚也；

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that for "flying" and "gu" without saying "came," they are generated by qi, what is called blight;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that for "flying" and "gu" without saying "came," they are generated by qi, what is called blight;

sentences.zh: 鴝鵒言來者，氣所致，所謂祥也。

translations.literal: for mynas, saying "came" means caused by qi, what is called portent.
translations.idiomatic: for mynas, saying "came" means caused by qi, what is called portent.

sentences.zh: 鴝鵒，夷狄穴藏之禽，來至中國，不穴而巢，陰居陽位，象季氏將逐昭公，去宮室而居外野也。

translations.literal: Mynas are birds that burrow and hide like barbarians. Coming to the central states and nesting instead of burrowing means yin occupying yang's place, symbolizing the Ji clan expelling Duke Zhao, who would leave palaces and live in open country.
translations.idiomatic: Mynas are birds that burrow and hide like barbarians. Coming to the central states and nesting instead of burrowing means yin occupying yang's place, symbolizing the Ji clan expelling Duke Zhao, who would leave palaces and live in open country.

sentences.zh: 鴝鵒白羽，旱之祥也；

translations.literal: Mynas have white feathers, a drought portent;
translations.idiomatic: Mynas have white feathers, a drought portent;

sentences.zh: 穴居而好水，黑色，為主急之應也。

translations.literal: they dwell in holes yet favor water, black in color, chiefly corresponding to haste and severity.
translations.idiomatic: they dwell in holes yet favor water, black in color, chiefly corresponding to haste and severity.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，既失眾，不可急暴；

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: since you have already lost the people, do not be harsh and violent;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: since you have already lost the people, do not be harsh and violent;

sentences.zh: 急暴，陰將持節陽以逐爾，去宮室而居外野矣。

translations.literal: if harsh and violent, yin will hold yang's authority to expel you, and you will leave palaces to live in the open country.
translations.idiomatic: if harsh and violent, yin will hold yang's authority to expel you, and you will leave palaces to live in the open country.

sentences.zh: 昭不寤，而舉兵圍季氏，為季氏所敗，出奔于齊，遂死于外野。

translations.literal: Zhao did not awaken, raised troops to besiege the Ji clan, was defeated by them, fled to Qi, and finally died in exile.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao did not awaken, raised troops to besiege the Ji clan, was defeated by them, fled to Qi, and finally died in exile.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒指略同。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.

sentences.zh: 景帝三年十一月，有白頸烏與黑烏群鬥楚國呂縣，白頸不勝，墮泗水中，死者數千。

translations.literal: In eleventh month of Emperor Jing year three, at Lü county in Chu, white-necked crows fought with black crows in groups. The white-necked lost and fell into the Si River; those dead numbered in the thousands.
translations.idiomatic: In eleventh month of Emperor Jing year three, at Lü county in Chu, white-necked crows fought with black crows in groups. The white-necked lost and fell into the Si River; those dead numbered in the thousands.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近白黑祥也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near white-black portent.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near white-black portent.

sentences.zh: 時楚王戊暴逆無道，刑辱申公，與吳王謀反。

translations.literal: At that time King Wu of Chu was violent and lawless, humiliating Shen Gong and plotting rebellion with King of Wu.
translations.idiomatic: At that time King Wu of Chu was violent and lawless, humiliating Shen Gong and plotting rebellion with King of Wu.

sentences.zh: 烏群鬥者，師戰之象也。

translations.literal: Flocks of crows fighting symbolize armies at war.
translations.idiomatic: Flocks of crows fighting symbolize armies at war.

sentences.zh: 白頸者小，明小者敗也。

translations.literal: The white-necked were fewer and smaller, showing the lesser side would lose.
translations.idiomatic: The white-necked were fewer and smaller, showing the lesser side would lose.

sentences.zh: 墮於水者，將死水地。

translations.literal: Falling into water meant he would die in a watery place.
translations.idiomatic: Falling into water meant he would die in a watery place.

sentences.zh: 王戊不寤，遂舉兵應吳，與漢大戰，兵敗而走，至於丹徒，為越人所斬，墮死於水之效也。

translations.literal: King Wu did not awaken and raised troops to join Wu. He fought Han, was defeated and fled, and at Dantu was beheaded by Yue men. This fulfilled the omen of falling to death in water.
translations.idiomatic: King Wu did not awaken and raised troops to join Wu. He fought Han, was defeated and fled, and at Dantu was beheaded by Yue men. This fulfilled the omen of falling to death in water.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「逆親親，厥妖白黑烏鬥於國。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "To oppose one's own kin: its portent is white and black crows fighting within the state."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "To oppose one's own kin: its portent is white and black crows fighting within the state."

sentences.zh: 昭帝時有鵜鶘或曰禿鶖，集昌邑王殿下，王使人射殺之。

translations.literal: In Emperor Zhao's time there were pelicans, or bald ospreys, gathering below the hall of the King of Changyi; the king had men shoot them.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Zhao's time there were pelicans, or bald ospreys, gathering below the hall of the King of Changyi; the king had men shoot them.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為水鳥色青，青祥也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took it as a blue portent, since water birds are blue-colored.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took it as a blue portent, since water birds are blue-colored.

sentences.zh: 時王馳騁無度，慢侮大臣，不敬至尊，有服妖之象，故青祥見也。

translations.literal: At that time the king rode recklessly without restraint, slighted great ministers, disrespected the highest authority, and had signs of clothing anomaly; therefore a blue portent appeared.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the king rode recklessly without restraint, slighted great ministers, disrespected the highest authority, and had signs of clothing anomaly; therefore a blue portent appeared.

sentences.zh: 野鳥入處，宮室將空。

translations.literal: When wild birds enter one's dwelling, palace chambers will be emptied.
translations.idiomatic: When wild birds enter one's dwelling, palace chambers will be emptied.

sentences.zh: 王不寤，卒以亡。

translations.literal: The king did not awaken and in the end perished.
translations.idiomatic: The king did not awaken and in the end perished.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「辟退有德，厥咎狂，厥妖水鳥集于國中。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When rulers dismiss the virtuous, its fault is madness; its portent is water birds gathering in the state."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When rulers dismiss the virtuous, its fault is madness; its portent is water birds gathering in the state."

sentences.zh: 鴻嘉二年三月，博士行大射禮，有飛雉集于庭，歷階登堂而雊。

translations.literal: In third month of Hongjia year two, during the grand archery rite performed by erudites, a flying pheasant gathered in the courtyard, climbed the steps, entered the hall, and crowed.
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Hongjia year two, during the grand archery rite performed by erudites, a flying pheasant gathered in the courtyard, climbed the steps, entered the hall, and crowed.

sentences.zh: 後雉又集太常、宗正、丞相、御史大夫、大司馬車騎將軍之府，又集未央宮承明殿屋上。

translations.literal: Later pheasants again gathered at the offices of the Minister of Ceremonies, Minister of the Imperial Clan, Chancellor, Censor-in-Chief, and the General-in-Chief of Chariots and Cavalry, and also on the roof of Chengming Hall in Weiyang Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Later pheasants again gathered at the offices of the Minister of Ceremonies, Minister of the Imperial Clan, Chancellor, Censor-in-Chief, and the General-in-Chief of Chariots and Cavalry, and also on the roof of Chengming Hall in Weiyang Palace.

sentences.zh: 時大司馬車騎將軍王音、待詔寵等上言：「天地之氣，以類相應，譴告人君，甚微而著。

translations.literal: At that time General-in-Chief Wang Yin and expectant appointee Chong and others memorialized: "The qi of Heaven and Earth responds by kind, admonishing the ruler, subtle yet manifest.
translations.idiomatic: At that time General-in-Chief Wang Yin and expectant appointee Chong and others memorialized: "The qi of Heaven and Earth responds by kind, admonishing the ruler, subtle yet manifest.

sentences.zh: 雉者聽察，先聞雷聲，故月令以紀氣。

translations.literal: Pheasants are birds of listening and watchfulness, first hearing thunder, so the Monthly Ordinances uses them to mark qi.
translations.idiomatic: Pheasants are birds of listening and watchfulness, first hearing thunder, so the Monthly Ordinances uses them to mark qi.

sentences.zh: 經載高宗雊雉之異，以明轉禍為福之驗。

translations.literal: The classics record Gaozong's anomaly of crowing pheasants to show the verification of turning disaster into blessing.
translations.idiomatic: The classics record Gaozong's anomaly of crowing pheasants to show the verification of turning disaster into blessing.

sentences.zh: 今雉以博士行禮之日大眾聚會，飛集於庭，歷階登堂，萬眾睢睢，驚怪連日。

translations.literal: Now pheasants, on the day erudites performed ritual and crowds assembled, flew to the courtyard, climbed steps, entered hall, with all the multitude staring in fear and wonder for days.
translations.idiomatic: Now pheasants, on the day erudites performed ritual and crowds assembled, flew to the courtyard, climbed steps, entered hall, with all the multitude staring in fear and wonder for days.

sentences.zh: 徑歷三公之府，太常宗正典宗廟骨肉之官，然後入宮。

translations.literal: They then passed through the offices of the Three Dukes; Taichang and Zongzheng are offices for ancestral temples and imperial kin, and only then entered the palace.
translations.idiomatic: They then passed through the offices of the Three Dukes; Taichang and Zongzheng are offices for ancestral temples and imperial kin, and only then entered the palace.

sentences.zh: 其宿留告曉人，具備深切，雖人道相戒，何以過是！」

translations.literal: Their lingering and announcing to people was complete and deeply urgent. Even if human guidance warned one another, what could exceed this?"
translations.idiomatic: Their lingering and announcing to people was complete and deeply urgent. Even if human guidance warned one another, what could exceed this?"

sentences.zh: 後帝使中常侍晁閎詔音曰：「聞捕得雉，毛羽頗摧折，類拘執者，得無人為之？」

translations.literal: Later the emperor sent middle attendant Chao Hong with an edict to Wang Yin: "I hear a pheasant was captured, with feathers much damaged as if seized. Could it have been man-made?"
translations.idiomatic: Later the emperor sent middle attendant Chao Hong with an edict to Wang Yin: "I hear a pheasant was captured, with feathers much damaged as if seized. Could it have been man-made?"

sentences.zh: 音復對曰：「陛下安得亡國之語？

translations.literal: Wang Yin replied again: "How could Your Majesty utter words of state ruin?
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yin replied again: "How could Your Majesty utter words of state ruin?

sentences.zh: 不知誰主為佞諂之計，誣亂聖德如此者！

translations.literal: I know not who devised this sycophantic plan, so slandering and confusing the sacred virtue!
translations.idiomatic: I know not who devised this sycophantic plan, so slandering and confusing the sacred virtue!

sentences.zh: 左右阿諛甚眾，不待臣音復諂而足。

translations.literal: Around Your Majesty there are many flatterers; no need for minister Yin to flatter further.
translations.idiomatic: Around Your Majesty there are many flatterers; no need for minister Yin to flatter further.

sentences.zh: 公卿以下，保位自守，莫有正言。

translations.literal: From high ministers downward all keep their positions and guard themselves; none speak upright words.
translations.idiomatic: From high ministers downward all keep their positions and guard themselves; none speak upright words.

sentences.zh: 如令陛下覺寤，懼大禍且至身，深責臣下，繩以聖法，臣音當先受誅，豈有以自解哉！

translations.literal: If Your Majesty were to awaken and fear great disaster near at hand, deeply reproaching ministers and binding them by sacred law, I, Yin, should be first to receive execution. How could I excuse myself?
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty were to awaken and fear great disaster near at hand, deeply reproaching ministers and binding them by sacred law, I, Yin, should be first to receive execution. How could I excuse myself?

sentences.zh: 今即位十五年，繼嗣不立，日日駕車而出，泆行流聞，海內傳之，甚於京師。

translations.literal: Now fifteen years on the throne, no heir established, and daily the imperial carriage goes out; reports of wanton roaming spread through the realm, even more than in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Now fifteen years on the throne, no heir established, and daily the imperial carriage goes out; reports of wanton roaming spread through the realm, even more than in the capital.

sentences.zh: 外有微行之害，內有疾病之憂，皇天數見災異，欲人變更，終已不改。

translations.literal: Outside there is harm from disguised excursions; inside there is worry of illness. August Heaven repeatedly shows disasters and anomalies wishing people to change, yet in the end there is no reform.
translations.idiomatic: Outside there is harm from disguised excursions; inside there is worry of illness. August Heaven repeatedly shows disasters and anomalies wishing people to change, yet in the end there is no reform.

sentences.zh: 天尚不能感動陛下，臣子何望？

translations.literal: If even Heaven cannot move Your Majesty, what can ministers hope for?
translations.idiomatic: If even Heaven cannot move Your Majesty, what can ministers hope for?

sentences.zh: 獨有極言待死，命在朝暮而已。

translations.literal: Only to speak to the utmost and await death, with life lasting morning to evening, that is all.
translations.idiomatic: Only to speak to the utmost and await death, with life lasting morning to evening, that is all.

sentences.zh: 如有不然，老母安得處所，尚何皇太后之有！

translations.literal: If this were not so, how could my aged mother have a place to dwell? What then of the Empress Dowager?
translations.idiomatic: If this were not so, how could my aged mother have a place to dwell? What then of the Empress Dowager?

sentences.zh: 高祖天下當以誰屬乎！

translations.literal: To whom should Gaozu's realm be entrusted?
translations.idiomatic: To whom should Gaozu's realm be entrusted?

sentences.zh: 宜謀於賢知，克己復禮，以求天意，繼嗣可立，災變尚可銷也。」

translations.literal: One should consult the wise and knowledgeable, overcome self and return to ritual, to seek Heaven's intent; then an heir may be established and disasters may still be dispelled."
translations.idiomatic: One should consult the wise and knowledgeable, overcome self and return to ritual, to seek Heaven's intent; then an heir may be established and disasters may still be dispelled."

sentences.zh: 成帝綏和二年三月，天水平襄有燕生爵，哺食至大，俱飛去。

translations.literal: In third month of Suihe year two under Emperor Cheng, in Pingxiang of Tianshui, swallows gave birth to sparrows; they were fed to full size and all flew away together.
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Suihe year two under Emperor Cheng, in Pingxiang of Tianshui, swallows gave birth to sparrows; they were fed to full size and all flew away together.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「賊臣在國，厥咎燕生爵，諸侯銷。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When traitor-ministers are in the state, its fault-sign is swallows giving birth to sparrows; the feudal lords are dissolved."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When traitor-ministers are in the state, its fault-sign is swallows giving birth to sparrows; the feudal lords are dissolved."

sentences.zh: 一曰，生非其類，子不嗣世。

translations.literal: Another view: giving birth to a different kind means sons will not continue the lineage.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: giving birth to a different kind means sons will not continue the lineage.

sentences.zh: 史記魯定公時，季桓子穿井，得土缶，中得蟲若羊，近羊禍也。

translations.literal: In the Records, in Duke Ding of Lu's time, Ji Huanzi dug a well and found an earthen jar; inside was a creature like a sheep, a near sheep calamity.
translations.idiomatic: In the Records, in Duke Ding of Lu's time, Ji Huanzi dug a well and found an earthen jar; inside was a creature like a sheep, a near sheep calamity.

sentences.zh: 羊者，地上之物，幽於土中，象定公不用孔子而聽季氏，暗昧不明之應也。

translations.literal: Sheep are creatures above ground yet hidden in earth, symbolizing Duke Ding's failure to employ Confucius and his listening to the Ji clan, a response of dimness and lack of clarity.
translations.idiomatic: Sheep are creatures above ground yet hidden in earth, symbolizing Duke Ding's failure to employ Confucius and his listening to the Ji clan, a response of dimness and lack of clarity.

sentences.zh: 一曰，羊去野外而拘土缶者，象魯君失其所而拘於季氏，季氏亦將拘於家臣也。

translations.literal: Another view: sheep leaving the wild and being confined in an earthen jar symbolizes the Lu ruler losing his proper place and being confined by the Ji clan; the Ji clan too would be confined by their own household ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: sheep leaving the wild and being confined in an earthen jar symbolizes the Lu ruler losing his proper place and being confined by the Ji clan; the Ji clan too would be confined by their own household ministers.

sentences.zh: 是歲季氏家臣陽虎囚季桓子。

translations.literal: That year Yang Hu, a Ji-clan household minister, imprisoned Ji Huanzi.
translations.idiomatic: That year Yang Hu, a Ji-clan household minister, imprisoned Ji Huanzi.

sentences.zh: 後三年，陽虎劫公伐孟氏，兵敗，竊寶玉大弓而出亡。

translations.literal: Three years later Yang Hu coerced the duke to attack the Meng clan, was defeated, stole treasured jade and great bow, and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later Yang Hu coerced the duke to attack the Meng clan, was defeated, stole treasured jade and great bow, and fled.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳魯襄公時，宋有生女子赤而毛，棄之隄下，宋平公母共姬之御者見而收之，因名曰棄。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says that in Duke Xiang of Lu's time, in Song there was born a girl red and hairy. She was abandoned below a dike. A charioteer of Gong Ji, mother of Duke Ping of Song, found and raised her, naming her Qi (Abandoned).
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says that in Duke Xiang of Lu's time, in Song there was born a girl red and hairy. She was abandoned below a dike. A charioteer of Gong Ji, mother of Duke Ping of Song, found and raised her, naming her Qi (Abandoned).

sentences.zh: 長而美好，納之平公，生子曰佐。

translations.literal: She grew up beautiful, was presented to Duke Ping, and bore a son named Zuo.
translations.idiomatic: She grew up beautiful, was presented to Duke Ping, and bore a son named Zuo.

sentences.zh: 後宋臣伊戾讒太子痤而殺之。

translations.literal: Later Song minister Yi Li slandered crown prince Cuo and had him killed.
translations.idiomatic: Later Song minister Yi Li slandered crown prince Cuo and had him killed.

sentences.zh: 先是，大夫華元出奔晉，華弱奔魯，華臣奔陳，華合比奔衛。

translations.literal: Before this, grandee Hua Yuan fled to Jin, Hua Ruo to Lu, Hua Chen to Chen, and Hua Hebi to Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, grandee Hua Yuan fled to Jin, Hua Ruo to Lu, Hua Chen to Chen, and Hua Hebi to Wei.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為時則火災赤眚之明應也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held this was the clear response of fire disaster and red blight.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this was the clear response of fire disaster and red blight.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「尊卑不別，厥妖女生赤毛。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When high and low are not distinguished, its portent is a girl born with red hair."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When high and low are not distinguished, its portent is a girl born with red hair."

sentences.zh: 惠帝二年，天雨血於宜陽，一頃所，劉向以為赤眚也。

translations.literal: In Emperor Hui year two, blood rained at Yiyang over about one qing of ground; Liu Xiang took it as a red blight.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Hui year two, blood rained at Yiyang over about one qing of ground; Liu Xiang took it as a red blight.

sentences.zh: 時又冬雷，桃李華，常奧之罰也。

translations.literal: That time there was also winter thunder and peaches and plums flowered, punishment of persistent heat.
translations.idiomatic: That time there was also winter thunder and peaches and plums flowered, punishment of persistent heat.

sentences.zh: 是時政舒緩，諸呂用事，讒口妄行，殺三皇子，建立非嗣，及不當立之王，退王陵、趙堯、周昌。

translations.literal: Governance then was lax; the Lü clan wielded power, slanderous tongues ran unchecked; three imperial princes were killed, improper heirs and kings were installed, and Wang Ling, Zhao Yao, and Zhou Chang were removed.
translations.idiomatic: Governance then was lax; the Lü clan wielded power, slanderous tongues ran unchecked; three imperial princes were killed, improper heirs and kings were installed, and Wang Ling, Zhao Yao, and Zhou Chang were removed.

sentences.zh: 呂太后崩，大臣共誅滅諸呂，僵尸流血。

translations.literal: When Empress Dowager Lü died, great ministers jointly exterminated the Lü clan, with fallen corpses and flowing blood.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Dowager Lü died, great ministers jointly exterminated the Lü clan, with fallen corpses and flowing blood.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「歸獄不解，茲謂追非，厥咎天雨血；

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When prison cases are not resolved and one pursues wrong, its blame-sign is blood raining from heaven;
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When prison cases are not resolved and one pursues wrong, its blame-sign is blood raining from heaven;

sentences.zh: 茲謂不親，民有怨心，不出三年，無其宗人。」

translations.literal: this is called lack of kinship; the people harbor resentment, and within three years their clan will be gone."
translations.idiomatic: this is called lack of kinship; the people harbor resentment, and within three years their clan will be gone."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「佞人祿，功臣僇，天雨血。」

translations.literal: It also says: "When sycophants are salaried and meritorious ministers are disgraced, heaven rains blood."
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "When sycophants are salaried and meritorious ministers are disgraced, heaven rains blood."

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平四年四月，山陽湖陵雨血，廣三尺，長五尺，大者如錢，小者如麻子。

translations.literal: In fourth month of Jianping year four under Emperor Ai, at Huling in Shanyang there was blood-rain, three chi wide and five chi long; larger drops were like coins, smaller like hemp seeds.
translations.idiomatic: In fourth month of Jianping year four under Emperor Ai, at Huling in Shanyang there was blood-rain, three chi wide and five chi long; larger drops were like coins, smaller like hemp seeds.

sentences.zh: 後二年，帝崩，王莽擅朝，誅貴戚丁、傅，大臣董賢等皆放徙遠方，與諸呂同眾。

translations.literal: Two years later the emperor died, Wang Mang monopolized court power, executed the noble Ding and Fu clans, and exiled great ministers such as Dong Xian to distant regions, similar in scale to the Lü case.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later the emperor died, Wang Mang monopolized court power, executed the noble Ding and Fu clans, and exiled great ministers such as Dong Xian to distant regions, similar in scale to the Lü case.

sentences.zh: 誅死者少，雨血亦少。

translations.literal: Since those executed were fewer, the blood-rain was also smaller.
translations.idiomatic: Since those executed were fewer, the blood-rain was also smaller.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「聽之不聰，是謂不謀，厥咎急，厥罰恆寒，厥極貧。

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "When hearing is not acute, this is called lack of planning; its blame is urgency, its punishment constant cold, and its extreme poverty.
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "When hearing is not acute, this is called lack of planning; its blame is urgency, its punishment constant cold, and its extreme poverty.

sentences.zh: 時則有鼓妖，時則有魚孽，時則有豕禍，時則有耳痾，時則有黑眚黑祥。

translations.literal: Then at times there are drum anomalies, at times fish prodigies, at times swine calamities, at times ear afflictions, and at times black blights and black portents.
translations.idiomatic: Then at times there are drum anomalies, at times fish prodigies, at times swine calamities, at times ear afflictions, and at times black blights and black portents.

sentences.zh: 惟火沴水。」

translations.literal: Only Fire afflicts Water."
translations.idiomatic: Only Fire afflicts Water."

sentences.zh: 桓公八年「十月，雨雪」。

translations.literal: In Duke Huan year eight: "In the tenth month, rain and snow."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Huan year eight: "In the tenth month, rain and snow."

sentences.zh: 周十月，今八月也，未可以雪，劉向以為時夫人有淫齊之行，而桓有妒媚之心，夫人將殺，其象見也。

translations.literal: Zhou tenth month is now eighth month, when snow should not yet occur. Liu Xiang held that then the consort had licentious conduct with Qi, and Huan had jealousy and flattery in mind; the consort would kill him, and the sign appeared first.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou tenth month is now eighth month, when snow should not yet occur. Liu Xiang held that then the consort had licentious conduct with Qi, and Huan had jealousy and flattery in mind; the consort would kill him, and the sign appeared first.

sentences.zh: 桓不覺寤，後與夫人俱如齊而殺死。

translations.literal: Huan did not awaken; later he and the consort both went to Qi and were killed.
translations.idiomatic: Huan did not awaken; later he and the consort both went to Qi and were killed.

sentences.zh: 凡雨，陰也，雪又雨之陰也，出非其時，迫近象也。

translations.literal: All rain is yin, and snow is even more yin within rain. Appearing out of season is an omen of imminent approach.
translations.idiomatic: All rain is yin, and snow is even more yin within rain. Appearing out of season is an omen of imminent approach.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為象大人專恣，陰氣盛也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held it symbolized great persons being arbitrary and yin-qi flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held it symbolized great persons being arbitrary and yin-qi flourishing.

sentences.zh: 釐公十年「冬，大雨雪」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year ten: "In winter, great rain and snow."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year ten: "In winter, great rain and snow."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是釐公立妾為夫人，陰居陽位，陰氣盛也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Xi had established a concubine as principal wife, yin occupying yang's position, so yin qi was excessive.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Xi had established a concubine as principal wife, yin occupying yang's position, so yin qi was excessive.

sentences.zh: 公羊經曰「大雨雹」。

translations.literal: The Gongyang Classic says: "Great rain and hail."
translations.idiomatic: The Gongyang Classic says: "Great rain and hail."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為公脅於齊桓公，立妾為夫人，不敢進群妾，故專壹之象見諸雹，皆為有所漸脅也，行專壹之政云。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that the duke, coerced by Duke Huan of Qi, made a concubine principal wife and did not dare advance the other concubines; therefore the sign of exclusivity appeared in hail. All showed gradual coercion and governance of exclusivity.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that the duke, coerced by Duke Huan of Qi, made a concubine principal wife and did not dare advance the other concubines; therefore the sign of exclusivity appeared in hail. All showed gradual coercion and governance of exclusivity.

sentences.zh: 昭公四年「正月，大雨雪」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhao year four: "In first month, great rain and snow."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year four: "In first month, great rain and snow."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為昭取於吳而為同姓，謂之吳孟子。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that Zhao took a wife from Wu and made her of same-surname rank, calling her Wu Mengzi.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that Zhao took a wife from Wu and made her of same-surname rank, calling her Wu Mengzi.

sentences.zh: 君行於上，臣非於下。

translations.literal: The ruler acted above, and ministers denounced below.
translations.idiomatic: The ruler acted above, and ministers denounced below.

sentences.zh: 又三家已彊，皆賤公行，慢臢之心生。

translations.literal: Moreover the Three Families were already strong, all despising the duke's conduct, with hearts of arrogance and contempt arising.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover the Three Families were already strong, all despising the duke's conduct, with hearts of arrogance and contempt arising.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為季孫宿任政，陰氣盛也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that Jisun Su held government, and yin qi was excessive.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that Jisun Su held government, and yin qi was excessive.

sentences.zh: 文帝四年六月，大雨雪。

translations.literal: In sixth month of Emperor Wen year four, there was great rain and snow.
translations.idiomatic: In sixth month of Emperor Wen year four, there was great rain and snow.

sentences.zh: 後三歲，淮南王長謀反，發覺，遷，道死。

translations.literal: Three years later, the King of Huainan, Chang, plotted rebellion; it was exposed, he was moved, and died on the road.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later, the King of Huainan, Chang, plotted rebellion; it was exposed, he was moved, and died on the road.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「夏雨雪，戒臣為亂。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Rain and snow in summer warns of ministers making disorder."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Rain and snow in summer warns of ministers making disorder."

sentences.zh: 景帝中六年三月，雨雪。

translations.literal: In third month of Emperor Jing, middle year six, there was rain and snow.
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Emperor Jing, middle year six, there was rain and snow.

sentences.zh: 其六月，匈奴入上郡取苑馬，吏卒戰死者二千餘人。

translations.literal: That sixth month, the Xiongnu entered Shang commandery and seized park horses; more than two thousand officials and soldiers died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: That sixth month, the Xiongnu entered Shang commandery and seized park horses; more than two thousand officials and soldiers died in battle.

sentences.zh: 明年，條侯周亞夫下獄死。

translations.literal: The next year, Marquis Tiao Zhou Yafu died in prison.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, Marquis Tiao Zhou Yafu died in prison.

sentences.zh: 武帝元狩元年十二月，大雨雪，民多凍死。

translations.literal: In twelfth month of Yuanshou year one under Emperor Wu, there was great rain and snow; many commoners froze to death.
translations.idiomatic: In twelfth month of Yuanshou year one under Emperor Wu, there was great rain and snow; many commoners froze to death.

sentences.zh: 是歲淮南、衡山王謀反，發覺，皆自殺。

translations.literal: That year the kings of Huainan and Hengshan plotted rebellion; exposed, both committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: That year the kings of Huainan and Hengshan plotted rebellion; exposed, both committed suicide.

sentences.zh: 使者行郡國，治黨與，坐死者數萬人。

translations.literal: Envoys traveled commanderies and kingdoms to prosecute accomplices; those condemned to death numbered in the tens of thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Envoys traveled commanderies and kingdoms to prosecute accomplices; those condemned to death numbered in the tens of thousands.

sentences.zh: 元鼎二年三月，雪，平地厚五尺。

translations.literal: In third month of Yuanding year two, there was snow, five chi deep on level ground.
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Yuanding year two, there was snow, five chi deep on level ground.

sentences.zh: 是歲御史大夫張湯有罪自殺，丞相嚴青翟坐與三長史謀陷湯，青翟自殺，三長史皆棄市。

translations.literal: That year Censor-in-Chief Zhang Tang, guilty, committed suicide; Chancellor Yan Qingdi was convicted of conspiring with three chief clerks to frame Zhang Tang, Qingdi killed himself, and the three chief clerks were all executed in the market.
translations.idiomatic: That year Censor-in-Chief Zhang Tang, guilty, committed suicide; Chancellor Yan Qingdi was convicted of conspiring with three chief clerks to frame Zhang Tang, Qingdi killed himself, and the three chief clerks were all executed in the market.

sentences.zh: 元鼎三年三月水冰，四月雨雪，關東十餘郡人相食。

translations.literal: In third month of Yuanding year three, waters froze; in fourth month rain and snow fell. In more than ten commanderies east of the pass, people ate one another.
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Yuanding year three, waters froze; in fourth month rain and snow fell. In more than ten commanderies east of the pass, people ate one another.

sentences.zh: 是歲，民不占緡錢有告者，以半畀之。

translations.literal: That year people who failed to report coin strings and were informed on had half confiscated and given to informants.
translations.idiomatic: That year people who failed to report coin strings and were informed on had half confiscated and given to informants.

sentences.zh: 建昭四年三月，雨雪，燕多死。

translations.literal: In third month of Jianzhao year four, rain and snow fell, and many swallows died.
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Jianzhao year four, rain and snow fell, and many swallows died.

sentences.zh: 谷永對曰：「皇后桑蠶以治祭服，共事天地宗廟，正以是日疾風自西北，大寒雨雪，壞敗其功，以章不鄉。

translations.literal: Gu Yong replied: "When the empress raises silkworms to make sacrificial garments and jointly serves Heaven, Earth, and ancestral temples, on that very day fierce wind from the northwest and severe cold rain-snow ruined the work to display disalignment.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong replied: "When the empress raises silkworms to make sacrificial garments and jointly serves Heaven, Earth, and ancestral temples, on that very day fierce wind from the northwest and severe cold rain-snow ruined the work to display disalignment.

sentences.zh: 宜齊戒辟寢，以深自責，請皇后就宮，鬲閉門戶，毋得擅上。

translations.literal: One should fast, abstain, and avoid the bedchamber, deeply blame oneself, and request the empress remain in her palace with doors closed, not allowed to come up at will.
translations.idiomatic: One should fast, abstain, and avoid the bedchamber, deeply blame oneself, and request the empress remain in her palace with doors closed, not allowed to come up at will.

sentences.zh: 且令眾妾人人更進，以時博施。

translations.literal: Also let the many consorts each advance in turn and bestow broadly in season.
translations.idiomatic: Also let the many consorts each advance in turn and bestow broadly in season.

sentences.zh: 皇天說喜，庶幾可以得賢明之嗣。

translations.literal: Then August Heaven may be pleased and perhaps a worthy enlightened heir may be obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Then August Heaven may be pleased and perhaps a worthy enlightened heir may be obtained.

sentences.zh: 即不行臣言，災異俞甚，天變成形，臣雖欲復捐身關策，不及事已。」

translations.literal: If my words are not carried out, disasters and anomalies will grow worse and Heaven's changes take concrete form; though I wish again to sacrifice myself with policy counsel, it will be too late."
translations.idiomatic: If my words are not carried out, disasters and anomalies will grow worse and Heaven's changes take concrete form; though I wish again to sacrifice myself with policy counsel, it will be too late."

sentences.zh: 其後許后坐祝詛廢。

translations.literal: Afterward Empress Xu was deposed on charges of cursing.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Empress Xu was deposed on charges of cursing.

sentences.zh: 陽朔四年四月，雨雪，燕雀死。

translations.literal: In fourth month of Yangshuo year four, rain and snow fell; swallows and sparrows died.
translations.idiomatic: In fourth month of Yangshuo year four, rain and snow fell; swallows and sparrows died.

sentences.zh: 後十六年，許皇后自殺。

translations.literal: Sixteen years later, Empress Xu committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Sixteen years later, Empress Xu committed suicide.

sentences.zh: 定公元年「十月，隕霜殺菽」。

translations.literal: In Duke Ding year one: "In the tenth month, falling frost killed beans."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ding year one: "In the tenth month, falling frost killed beans."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為周十月，今八月也，消卦為觀，陰氣未至君位而殺，誅罰不由君出，在臣下之象也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that Zhou tenth month is now eighth month; the dissipating hexagram is Viewing. Yin qi had not yet reached ruler position but already killed, meaning punishments not issued by the ruler but by ministers below.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that Zhou tenth month is now eighth month; the dissipating hexagram is Viewing. Yin qi had not yet reached ruler position but already killed, meaning punishments not issued by the ruler but by ministers below.

sentences.zh: 是時季氏逐昭公，公死于外，定公得立，故天見災以視公也。

translations.literal: At that time the Ji clan had expelled Duke Zhao and he died abroad; Duke Ding was installed, so Heaven showed disaster to warn the duke.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the Ji clan had expelled Duke Zhao and he died abroad; Duke Ding was installed, so Heaven showed disaster to warn the duke.

sentences.zh: 釐公二年「十月，隕霜不殺草」，為嗣君微，失秉事之象也。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year two: "In the tenth month, falling frost did not kill grass," symbolizing a weak heir-ruler and loss of authority over affairs.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year two: "In the tenth month, falling frost did not kill grass," symbolizing a weak heir-ruler and loss of authority over affairs.

sentences.zh: 其後卒在臣下，則災為之生矣。

translations.literal: Afterward power finally lay with ministers below, and so disaster arose from it.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward power finally lay with ministers below, and so disaster arose from it.

sentences.zh: 異故言草，災故言菽，重殺穀。

translations.literal: For anomaly it says grass; for disaster it says beans, emphasizing grain being killed.
translations.idiomatic: For anomaly it says grass; for disaster it says beans, emphasizing grain being killed.

sentences.zh: 一曰菽，草之難殺者也，言殺菽，知草皆死也；

translations.literal: Another view: beans are among the grasses hardest to kill; saying beans were killed means grasses all died.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: beans are among the grasses hardest to kill; saying beans were killed means grasses all died.

sentences.zh: 言不殺草，知菽亦不死也。
