translations.literal: Saying grass was not killed means beans also did not die.
translations.idiomatic: Saying grass was not killed means beans also did not die.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為菽，草之彊者，天戒若曰，加誅於彊臣。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held beans are the stronger grasses; Heaven's warning was as if saying: add punishments against powerful ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held beans are the stronger grasses; Heaven's warning was as if saying: add punishments against powerful ministers.

sentences.zh: 言菽，以微見季氏之罰也。

translations.literal: Speaking of beans subtly showed punishment of the Ji clan.
translations.idiomatic: Speaking of beans subtly showed punishment of the Ji clan.

sentences.zh: 武帝元光四年四月，隕霜殺草木。

translations.literal: In fourth month of Yuanguang year four under Emperor Wu, falling frost killed grasses and trees.
translations.idiomatic: In fourth month of Yuanguang year four under Emperor Wu, falling frost killed grasses and trees.

sentences.zh: 先是二年，遣五將軍三十萬眾伏馬邑下，欲襲單于，單于覺之而去。

translations.literal: Two years before this, five generals with 300,000 troops were sent to lie in ambush below Mayi intending to strike the Chanyu, but the Chanyu detected it and withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: Two years before this, five generals with 300,000 troops were sent to lie in ambush below Mayi intending to strike the Chanyu, but the Chanyu detected it and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 自是始征伐四夷，師出三十餘年，天下戶口減半。

translations.literal: From then began campaigns against the four border peoples; armies were sent for over thirty years, and household population of the realm was halved.
translations.idiomatic: From then began campaigns against the four border peoples; armies were sent for over thirty years, and household population of the realm was halved.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「興兵妄誅，茲謂亡法，厥災霜，夏殺五穀，冬殺麥。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Raising troops and punishing recklessly is called lawlessness; its disaster is frost, killing the five grains in summer and killing wheat in winter.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Raising troops and punishing recklessly is called lawlessness; its disaster is frost, killing the five grains in summer and killing wheat in winter.

sentences.zh: 誅不原情，茲謂不仁，其霜，夏先大雷風，冬先雨，乃隕霜，有芒角。

translations.literal: Punishing without considering circumstances is called unbenevolence; then before the frost in summer there are great thunder and wind, before winter there is rain, then falling frost with barbs and points.
translations.idiomatic: Punishing without considering circumstances is called unbenevolence; then before the frost in summer there are great thunder and wind, before winter there is rain, then falling frost with barbs and points.

sentences.zh: 賢聖遭害，其霜附木不下地。

translations.literal: When worthy sages are harmed, frost clings to trees and does not fall to the ground.
translations.idiomatic: When worthy sages are harmed, frost clings to trees and does not fall to the ground.

sentences.zh: 佞人依刑，茲謂私賊，其霜在草根土隙間。

translations.literal: When sycophants rely on punishments, this is called private predation; frost appears among grass roots and soil cracks.
translations.idiomatic: When sycophants rely on punishments, this is called private predation; frost appears among grass roots and soil cracks.

sentences.zh: 不教而誅茲謂虐，其霜反在草下。」

translations.literal: Punishing without teaching is called cruelty; frost instead appears below the grass."
translations.idiomatic: Punishing without teaching is called cruelty; frost instead appears below the grass."

sentences.zh: 元帝永光元年三月，隕霜殺桑；

translations.literal: In third month of Yongguang year one under Emperor Yuan, falling frost killed mulberry;
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Yongguang year one under Emperor Yuan, falling frost killed mulberry;

sentences.zh: 九月二日，隕霜殺稼，天下大飢。

translations.literal: on the second day of ninth month, falling frost killed crops, and there was great famine under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: on the second day of ninth month, falling frost killed crops, and there was great famine under heaven.

sentences.zh: 是時中書令石顯用事專權，與春秋定公時隕霜同應。

translations.literal: At that time Secretariat Director Shi Xian wielded power and monopolized authority, corresponding with the falling frost in Duke Ding's time in Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Secretariat Director Shi Xian wielded power and monopolized authority, corresponding with the falling frost in Duke Ding's time in Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 成帝即位，顯坐作威福誅。

translations.literal: When Emperor Cheng took the throne, Shi Xian was executed for exercising arbitrary power over rewards and punishments.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Cheng took the throne, Shi Xian was executed for exercising arbitrary power over rewards and punishments.

sentences.zh: 釐公二十九年「秋，大雨雹」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year twenty-nine: "In autumn, great rain and hail."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year twenty-nine: "In autumn, great rain and hail."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為盛陽雨水，溫煖而湯熱，陰氣脅之不相入，則轉而為雹；

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that when flourishing yang brings rainwater, warm and hot, and yin qi presses it without joining, it turns into hail;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that when flourishing yang brings rainwater, warm and hot, and yin qi presses it without joining, it turns into hail;

sentences.zh: 盛陰雨雪，凝滯而冰寒，陽氣薄之不相入，則散而為霰。

translations.literal: when flourishing yin brings rain and snow, congealed and icy cold, and yang qi presses without joining, it disperses into sleet.
translations.idiomatic: when flourishing yin brings rain and snow, congealed and icy cold, and yang qi presses without joining, it disperses into sleet.

sentences.zh: 故沸湯之在閉器，而湛於寒泉，則為冰，及雪之銷，亦冰解而散，此其驗也。

translations.literal: Thus boiling water in a closed vessel, immersed in a cold spring, turns to ice; and when snow melts, ice too breaks and scatters. This verifies it.
translations.idiomatic: Thus boiling water in a closed vessel, immersed in a cold spring, turns to ice; and when snow melts, ice too breaks and scatters. This verifies it.

sentences.zh: 故雹者陰脅陽也，霰者陽脅陰也，春秋不書霰者，猶月食也。

translations.literal: Therefore hail is yin coercing yang, while sleet is yang coercing yin. Spring and Autumn does not record sleet, just as with monthly eclipses.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore hail is yin coercing yang, while sleet is yang coercing yin. Spring and Autumn does not record sleet, just as with monthly eclipses.

sentences.zh: 釐公末年信用公子遂，遂專權自恣，將至於殺君，故陰脅陽之象見。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi's final years he trusted Prince Sui, who monopolized power and indulged himself, coming to the point of killing rulers; thus appeared the sign of yin coercing yang.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi's final years he trusted Prince Sui, who monopolized power and indulged himself, coming to the point of killing rulers; thus appeared the sign of yin coercing yang.

sentences.zh: 釐公不寤，遂終專權，後二年殺子赤，立宣公。

translations.literal: Duke Xi did not awaken; Sui ended by fully monopolizing power and two years later killed Zichi and established Duke Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Xi did not awaken; Sui ended by fully monopolizing power and two years later killed Zichi and established Duke Xuan.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰：「聖人在上無雹，雖有不為災。」

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: "When sages are above, there is no hail; even if there is, it is not disaster."
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says: "When sages are above, there is no hail; even if there is, it is not disaster."

sentences.zh: 說曰：凡物不為災不書，書大，言為災也。

translations.literal: The explanation says: whatever does not constitute disaster is not recorded; recording "great" means it was disaster.
translations.idiomatic: The explanation says: whatever does not constitute disaster is not recorded; recording "great" means it was disaster.

sentences.zh: 凡雹，皆冬之愆陽，夏之伏陰也。

translations.literal: All hail is errant yang in winter and latent yin in summer.
translations.idiomatic: All hail is errant yang in winter and latent yin in summer.

sentences.zh: 昭公三年，「大雨雹」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhao year three: "Great rain and hail."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year three: "Great rain and hail."

sentences.zh: 是時季氏專權，脅君之象見。

translations.literal: At that time the Ji clan monopolized power, and the sign of coercing the ruler appeared.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the Ji clan monopolized power, and the sign of coercing the ruler appeared.

sentences.zh: 昭公不寤，後季氏卒逐昭公。

translations.literal: Duke Zhao did not awaken; later the Ji clan finally expelled him.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Zhao did not awaken; later the Ji clan finally expelled him.

sentences.zh: 元封三年十二月，雷雨雹，大如馬頭。

translations.literal: In twelfth month of Yuanfeng year three, there was thunder, rain, and hail as large as horse heads.
translations.idiomatic: In twelfth month of Yuanfeng year three, there was thunder, rain, and hail as large as horse heads.

sentences.zh: 宣帝地節四年五月，山陽濟陰雨雹如雞子，深二尺五寸，殺二十人，蜚鳥皆死。

translations.literal: In fifth month of Dijie year four under Emperor Xuan, in Shanyang and Jiyin hail fell like chicken eggs, 2 chi 5 cun deep, killing twenty people; all flying birds died.
translations.idiomatic: In fifth month of Dijie year four under Emperor Xuan, in Shanyang and Jiyin hail fell like chicken eggs, 2 chi 5 cun deep, killing twenty people; all flying birds died.

sentences.zh: 其十月，大司馬霍禹宗族謀反，誅，霍皇后廢。

translations.literal: That tenth month, Grand Marshal Huo Yu's clan plotted rebellion and was executed, and Empress Huo was deposed.
translations.idiomatic: That tenth month, Grand Marshal Huo Yu's clan plotted rebellion and was executed, and Empress Huo was deposed.

sentences.zh: 成帝河平二年四月，楚國雨雹，大如斧，蜚鳥死。

translations.literal: In fourth month of Heping year two under Emperor Cheng, hail fell in Chu state, as large as axes, and flying birds died.
translations.idiomatic: In fourth month of Heping year two under Emperor Cheng, hail fell in Chu state, as large as axes, and flying birds died.

sentences.zh: 左傳曰釐公三十二年十二月己卯，晉文公卒，庚辰，將殯于曲沃，出絳，柩有聲如牛。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: in Duke Xi year thirty-two, twelfth month, jimao day, Duke Wen of Jin died. On gengchen day, as they prepared to encoffin him at Quwo and left Jiang, the coffin made a sound like an ox.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says: in Duke Xi year thirty-two, twelfth month, jimao day, Duke Wen of Jin died. On gengchen day, as they prepared to encoffin him at Quwo and left Jiang, the coffin made a sound like an ox.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近鼓妖也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near drum anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near drum anomaly.

sentences.zh: 喪，凶事；

translations.literal: Mourning is an inauspicious affair;
translations.idiomatic: Mourning is an inauspicious affair;

sentences.zh: 聲如牛，怒象也。

translations.literal: a sound like an ox is an image of anger.
translations.idiomatic: a sound like an ox is an image of anger.

sentences.zh: 將有急怒之謀，以生兵革之禍。

translations.literal: There would be a scheme of urgent rage producing disaster of warfare.
translations.idiomatic: There would be a scheme of urgent rage producing disaster of warfare.

sentences.zh: 是時，秦穆公遣兵襲鄭而不假道，還，晉大夫先軫謂襄公曰，秦師過不假塗，請擊之。

translations.literal: At that time Duke Mu of Qin sent troops to raid Zheng without borrowing passage. On return, Jin grandee Xian Zhen told Duke Xiang: Qin forces passed without borrowing road; please strike them.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Mu of Qin sent troops to raid Zheng without borrowing passage. On return, Jin grandee Xian Zhen told Duke Xiang: Qin forces passed without borrowing road; please strike them.

sentences.zh: 遂要崤阨，以敗秦師，匹馬觭輪無反者，操之急矣。

translations.literal: So they blocked the Xiao pass and defeated Qin forces; not even a single horse or half a wheel returned. Their action was extremely urgent.
translations.idiomatic: So they blocked the Xiao pass and defeated Qin forces; not even a single horse or half a wheel returned. Their action was extremely urgent.

sentences.zh: 晉不惟舊，而聽虐謀，結怨彊國，四被秦寇，禍流數世，凶惡之效也。

translations.literal: Jin did not uphold old ties but listened to cruel plans, forging resentment with a powerful state. It was invaded by Qin four times, calamity flowing across generations: the effect of inauspicious evil.
translations.idiomatic: Jin did not uphold old ties but listened to cruel plans, forging resentment with a powerful state. It was invaded by Qin four times, calamity flowing across generations: the effect of inauspicious evil.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平二年四月乙亥朔，御史大夫朱博為丞相，少府趙玄為御史大夫，臨延登受策，有大聲如鍾鳴，殿中郎吏陛者皆聞焉。

translations.literal: On yihai first day of fourth month, Jianping year two under Emperor Ai, Zhu Bo became chancellor and Zhao Xuan became Censor-in-Chief. As they approached the hall and ascended to receive edicts, there was a great sound like a bell; all palace attendants on the steps heard it.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai first day of fourth month, Jianping year two under Emperor Ai, Zhu Bo became chancellor and Zhao Xuan became Censor-in-Chief. As they approached the hall and ascended to receive edicts, there was a great sound like a bell; all palace attendants on the steps heard it.

sentences.zh: 上以問黃門侍郎揚雄、李尋，尋對曰：「洪範所謂鼓妖者也。

translations.literal: The emperor asked Yellow Gate attendants Yang Xiong and Li Xun; Xun replied: "This is what the Great Plan calls a drum anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked Yellow Gate attendants Yang Xiong and Li Xun; Xun replied: "This is what the Great Plan calls a drum anomaly.

sentences.zh: 師法以為人君不聰，為眾所惑，空名得進，則有聲無形，不知所從生。

translations.literal: By authoritative teaching, if the ruler is not discerning and is deluded by the multitude, and empty reputations advance, then there is sound without form and one cannot know whence it comes.
translations.idiomatic: By authoritative teaching, if the ruler is not discerning and is deluded by the multitude, and empty reputations advance, then there is sound without form and one cannot know whence it comes.

sentences.zh: 其傳曰歲月日之中，則正卿受之。

translations.literal: Its transmission says that in the center of year, month, and day, then the chief minister receives it.
translations.idiomatic: Its transmission says that in the center of year, month, and day, then the chief minister receives it.

sentences.zh: 今以四月日加辰巳有異，是為中焉。

translations.literal: Now in fourth month the day falls on chen-si and there is anomaly; this counts as center.
translations.idiomatic: Now in fourth month the day falls on chen-si and there is anomaly; this counts as center.

sentences.zh: 正卿謂執政大臣也。

translations.literal: Chief minister means the governing great ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Chief minister means the governing great ministers.

sentences.zh: 宜退丞相、御史，以應天變。

translations.literal: The chancellor and censor should be removed to answer Heaven's change.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor and censor should be removed to answer Heaven's change.

sentences.zh: 然雖不退，不出期年，其人自蒙其咎。」

translations.literal: Yet even if not removed, within less than a year those persons will themselves bear their blame."
translations.idiomatic: Yet even if not removed, within less than a year those persons will themselves bear their blame."

sentences.zh: 揚雄亦以為鼓妖，聽失之象也。

translations.literal: Yang Xiong likewise took it as drum anomaly, image of failed hearing.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xiong likewise took it as drum anomaly, image of failed hearing.

sentences.zh: 朱博為人彊毅多權謀，宜將不宜相，恐有凶惡亟疾之怒。

translations.literal: Zhu Bo was strong-willed and full of stratagems, fit to command armies not to be chancellor; I fear fierce and sudden wrathful disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo was strong-willed and full of stratagems, fit to command armies not to be chancellor; I fear fierce and sudden wrathful disaster.

sentences.zh: 八月，博、玄坐為姦謀，博自殺，玄減死論。

translations.literal: In eighth month, Bo and Xuan were convicted of treacherous plotting; Bo killed himself and Xuan had sentence reduced from death.
translations.idiomatic: In eighth month, Bo and Xuan were convicted of treacherous plotting; Bo killed himself and Xuan had sentence reduced from death.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「令不修本，下不安，金毋故自動，若有音。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When orders do not repair the root and those below are uneasy, metal moves by itself without cause, as if making sound."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When orders do not repair the root and those below are uneasy, metal moves by itself without cause, as if making sound."

sentences.zh: 史記秦始皇八年，河魚大上。

translations.literal: In the Records, in year eight of First Emperor of Qin, river fish rose greatly upstream.
translations.idiomatic: In the Records, in year eight of First Emperor of Qin, river fish rose greatly upstream.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近魚孽也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took it as a near fish prodigy.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took it as a near fish prodigy.

sentences.zh: 是歲，始皇弟長安君將兵擊趙，反，死屯留，軍吏皆斬，遷其民於臨洮。

translations.literal: That year the First Emperor's younger brother, Lord Chang'an, led troops to strike Zhao, then rebelled and died at Tunliu; military officials were all beheaded and his people moved to Lintao.
translations.idiomatic: That year the First Emperor's younger brother, Lord Chang'an, led troops to strike Zhao, then rebelled and died at Tunliu; military officials were all beheaded and his people moved to Lintao.

sentences.zh: 明年有嫪毒之誅。

translations.literal: The next year came the punishment of Lao Ai.
translations.idiomatic: The next year came the punishment of Lao Ai.

sentences.zh: 魚陰類，民之象，逆流而上者，民將不從君令為逆行也。

translations.literal: Fish are yin kinds and symbolize the people; swimming upstream means people will not obey ruler's commands and will act rebelliously.
translations.idiomatic: Fish are yin kinds and symbolize the people; swimming upstream means people will not obey ruler's commands and will act rebelliously.

sentences.zh: 其在天文，魚星中河而處，車騎滿野。

translations.literal: In astronomy, the Fish star lies in the River sector, with chariots and cavalry filling the wilds.
translations.idiomatic: In astronomy, the Fish star lies in the River sector, with chariots and cavalry filling the wilds.

sentences.zh: 至于二世，暴虐愈甚，終用急亡。

translations.literal: By the time of Second Emperor, tyranny was even worse and in the end he perished through harshness.
translations.idiomatic: By the time of Second Emperor, tyranny was even worse and in the end he perished through harshness.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「眾逆同志，厥妖河魚逆流上。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the masses rebel with one intent, its portent is river fish swimming upstream."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the masses rebel with one intent, its portent is river fish swimming upstream."

sentences.zh: 武帝元鼎五年秋，蛙與蝦蟆群鬥。

translations.literal: In autumn of Yuanding year five under Emperor Wu, frogs and toads fought in groups.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Yuanding year five under Emperor Wu, frogs and toads fought in groups.

sentences.zh: 是歲，四將軍眾十萬征南越，開九郡。

translations.literal: That year four generals with 100,000 troops campaigned against Nanyue and opened nine commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: That year four generals with 100,000 troops campaigned against Nanyue and opened nine commanderies.

sentences.zh: 成帝鴻嘉四年秋，雨魚于信都，長五寸以下。

translations.literal: In autumn of Hongjia year four under Emperor Cheng, fish rained down at Xindu, each under five cun long.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Hongjia year four under Emperor Cheng, fish rained down at Xindu, each under five cun long.

sentences.zh: 成帝永始元年春，北海出大魚，長六丈，高一丈，四枚。

translations.literal: In spring of Yongshi year one under Emperor Cheng, huge fish appeared in Beihai, six zhang long and one zhang high, four in number.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of Yongshi year one under Emperor Cheng, huge fish appeared in Beihai, six zhang long and one zhang high, four in number.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平三年，東萊平度出大魚，長八丈，高丈一尺，七枚，皆死。

translations.literal: In Jianping year three under Emperor Ai, huge fish appeared at Pingdu in Donglai, eight zhang long and one zhang one chi high, seven in number, all dead.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianping year three under Emperor Ai, huge fish appeared at Pingdu in Donglai, eight zhang long and one zhang one chi high, seven in number, all dead.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「海數見巨魚，邪人進，賢人疏。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the sea repeatedly shows giant fish, wicked men advance and worthy men are kept distant."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the sea repeatedly shows giant fish, wicked men advance and worthy men are kept distant."

sentences.zh: 桓公五年「秋，螽」。

translations.literal: In Duke Huan year five: "In autumn, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Huan year five: "In autumn, locusts."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為貪虐取民則螽，介蟲之孽也，與魚同占。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that greedy tyranny and exactions from the people bring locusts, a prodigy of armored insects, with the same divinatory meaning as fish.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that greedy tyranny and exactions from the people bring locusts, a prodigy of armored insects, with the same divinatory meaning as fish.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為介蟲之孽屬言不從。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that prodigies of armored insects belong to speech not being heeded.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that prodigies of armored insects belong to speech not being heeded.

sentences.zh: 是歲，公獲二國之聘，取鼎易邑，興役起城。

translations.literal: That year the duke received missions from two states, seized tripods and exchanged towns, and mobilized labor to build walls.
translations.idiomatic: That year the duke received missions from two states, seized tripods and exchanged towns, and mobilized labor to build walls.

sentences.zh: 諸螽略皆從董仲舒說云。

translations.literal: Most locust entries follow Dong Zhongshu's explanation.
translations.idiomatic: Most locust entries follow Dong Zhongshu's explanation.

sentences.zh: 嚴公二十九年「有蜚」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhuang year twenty-nine: "There were fei insects."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year twenty-nine: "There were fei insects."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為負蠜也，性不食穀，食穀為災，介蟲之孽。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held these were burden-carrying beetles, naturally not grain-eating; when they eat grain and cause disaster, it is a prodigy of armored insects.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held these were burden-carrying beetles, naturally not grain-eating; when they eat grain and cause disaster, it is a prodigy of armored insects.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為蜚色青，近青眚也，非中國所有。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that fei are blue in color, a near blue blight, and not native to the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that fei are blue in color, a near blue blight, and not native to the central states.

sentences.zh: 南越盛暑，男女同川澤，淫風所生，為蟲臭惡。

translations.literal: In the great heat of Nanyue, men and women share rivers and marshes; from licentious customs arise these foul-smelling insects.
translations.idiomatic: In the great heat of Nanyue, men and women share rivers and marshes; from licentious customs arise these foul-smelling insects.

sentences.zh: 是時嚴公取齊淫女為夫人，既入，淫於兩叔，故蜚至。

translations.literal: At that time Duke Zhuang took a licentious woman of Qi as wife. After entering, she had illicit relations with two uncles, and so fei arrived.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Zhuang took a licentious woman of Qi as wife. After entering, she had illicit relations with two uncles, and so fei arrived.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，今誅絕之尚及，不將生臭惡，聞於四方。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: if you execute and cut this off now, it is still in time; otherwise foul evil will spread and be heard in all directions.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: if you execute and cut this off now, it is still in time; otherwise foul evil will spread and be heard in all directions.

sentences.zh: 嚴不寤，其後夫人與兩叔作亂，二嗣以殺，卒皆被辜。

translations.literal: Zhuang did not awaken. Later the wife and two uncles made disorder, two heirs were killed, and all eventually bore punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuang did not awaken. Later the wife and two uncles made disorder, two heirs were killed, and all eventually bore punishment.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒指略同。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.

sentences.zh: 釐公十五年「八月，螽」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year fifteen: "In the eighth month, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year fifteen: "In the eighth month, locusts."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是釐有鹹之會，後城緣陵，是歲復以兵車為牡丘會，使公孫敖帥師，及諸侯大夫救徐，兵比三年在外。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Xi had the Xian conference, then fortified Yuanyling; that year he again used war chariots for the Muqiu conference, sent Gongsun Ao to lead troops, and together with feudal officers rescued Xu. For three straight years troops stayed abroad.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Xi had the Xian conference, then fortified Yuanyling; that year he again used war chariots for the Muqiu conference, sent Gongsun Ao to lead troops, and together with feudal officers rescued Xu. For three straight years troops stayed abroad.

sentences.zh: 文公三年「秋，雨螽于宋」。

translations.literal: In Duke Wen year three: "In autumn, locusts rained in Song."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Wen year three: "In autumn, locusts rained in Song."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是宋殺大夫而無罪，有暴虐賦斂之應。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this Song killed a grandee without guilt, a response to violent exactions and tyranny.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this Song killed a grandee without guilt, a response to violent exactions and tyranny.

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰上下皆合，言甚。

translations.literal: The Guliang Tradition says all above and below were implicated, meaning severity.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Tradition says all above and below were implicated, meaning severity.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為宋三世內取，大夫專恣，殺生不中，故螽先死而至。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that in Song, for three generations internal usurpations occurred, grandees acted arbitrarily, and killing and sparing were improper; therefore locusts first died and then arrived.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that in Song, for three generations internal usurpations occurred, grandees acted arbitrarily, and killing and sparing were improper; therefore locusts first died and then arrived.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為螽為穀災，卒遇賊陰，墜而死也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that locusts are grain-disaster; they happened to meet hostile yin, fell, and died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that locusts are grain-disaster; they happened to meet hostile yin, fell, and died.

sentences.zh: 八年「十月，螽」。

translations.literal: In year eight: "In the tenth month, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: In year eight: "In the tenth month, locusts."

sentences.zh: 時公伐邾取須朐，城郚。

translations.literal: At that time the duke attacked Zhu and seized Xuqu, and fortified Wu.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the duke attacked Zhu and seized Xuqu, and fortified Wu.

sentences.zh: 宣公六年「八月，螽」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xuan year six: "In the eighth month, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xuan year six: "In the eighth month, locusts."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是時宣伐莒向，後比再如齊，謀伐萊。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Xuan attacked Ju and Xiang, then repeatedly went to Qi to plan attacking Lai.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Xuan attacked Ju and Xiang, then repeatedly went to Qi to plan attacking Lai.

sentences.zh: 十三年「秋，螽」。

translations.literal: In year thirteen: "In autumn, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: In year thirteen: "In autumn, locusts."

sentences.zh: 公孫歸父會齊伐莒。

translations.literal: Gongsun Guifu joined Qi in attacking Ju.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Guifu joined Qi in attacking Ju.

sentences.zh: 十五年「秋，螽」。

translations.literal: In year fifteen: "In autumn, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: In year fifteen: "In autumn, locusts."

sentences.zh: 宣亡熟歲，數有軍旅。

translations.literal: In Xuan's years there were no good harvests, and military campaigns were frequent.
translations.idiomatic: In Xuan's years there were no good harvests, and military campaigns were frequent.

sentences.zh: 襄公七年「八月，螽」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xiang year seven: "In the eighth month, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang year seven: "In the eighth month, locusts."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是襄興師救陳，滕子、郯子、小邾子皆來朝。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this Xiang raised troops to rescue Chen, and the rulers of Teng, Tan, and Lesser Zhu all came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this Xiang raised troops to rescue Chen, and the rulers of Teng, Tan, and Lesser Zhu all came to court.

sentences.zh: 夏，城費。

translations.literal: In summer, they fortified Fei.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, they fortified Fei.

sentences.zh: 哀公十二年「十二月，螽」。

translations.literal: In Duke Ai year twelve: "In the twelfth month, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai year twelve: "In the twelfth month, locusts."

sentences.zh: 是時哀用田賦。

translations.literal: At that time Duke Ai imposed field taxes.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Ai imposed field taxes.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為春用田賦，冬而螽。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that field taxes were used in spring, and locusts came in winter.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that field taxes were used in spring, and locusts came in winter.

sentences.zh: 十三年「九月，螽；

translations.literal: In year thirteen: "In the ninth month, locusts;
translations.idiomatic: In year thirteen: "In the ninth month, locusts;

sentences.zh: 十二月，螽」。

translations.literal: in the twelfth month, locusts."
translations.idiomatic: in the twelfth month, locusts."

sentences.zh: 比三螽，虐取於民之效也。

translations.literal: Three consecutive locust events were the effect of oppressive extraction from the people.
translations.idiomatic: Three consecutive locust events were the effect of oppressive extraction from the people.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為周十二月，夏十月也，火星既伏，蟄蟲皆畢，天之見變，因物類之宜，不得以螽，是歲再失閏矣。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that Zhou twelfth month is Xia tenth month; once Mars is hidden and hibernating insects are all complete, Heaven's displayed changes should match suitable kinds, so it should not be locusts. That year intercalation was missed twice.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that Zhou twelfth month is Xia tenth month; once Mars is hidden and hibernating insects are all complete, Heaven's displayed changes should match suitable kinds, so it should not be locusts. That year intercalation was missed twice.

sentences.zh: 周九月，夏七月，故傳曰「火猶西流，司曆過也」。

translations.literal: Zhou ninth month is Xia seventh month, therefore the Tradition says, "Fire still flows westward; the calendar officers erred."
translations.idiomatic: Zhou ninth month is Xia seventh month, therefore the Tradition says, "Fire still flows westward; the calendar officers erred."

sentences.zh: 宣公十五年「冬，蝝生」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xuan year fifteen: "In winter, larvae were born."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xuan year fifteen: "In winter, larvae were born."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為蝝，鐵缨之有翼者，食穀為災，黑眚也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that yuan larvae are winged iron-wing insects that eat grain and cause disaster, a black blight.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that yuan larvae are winged iron-wing insects that eat grain and cause disaster, a black blight.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為蝝，螟始生也，一曰

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that yuan are newly born borers; another view says
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that yuan are newly born borers; another view says

sentences.zh: 螟始生。

translations.literal: newly born borers.
translations.idiomatic: newly born borers.

sentences.zh: 是時民患上力役，解於公田。

translations.literal: At that time the people suffered from state labor demands and neglected public fields.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the people suffered from state labor demands and neglected public fields.

sentences.zh: 宣是時初稅畝。

translations.literal: It was then that Duke Xuan first imposed tax per mu.
translations.idiomatic: It was then that Duke Xuan first imposed tax per mu.

sentences.zh: 稅畝，就民田畝擇美者稅其什一，亂先王制而為貪利，故應是而蝝生，屬蠃蟲之孽。

translations.literal: Tax-per-mu meant selecting the best parts of people's fields and taxing one-tenth, disrupting former kings' system for greedy profit; therefore this response appeared and larvae were born, belonging to prodigies of shell-creatures.
translations.idiomatic: Tax-per-mu meant selecting the best parts of people's fields and taxing one-tenth, disrupting former kings' system for greedy profit; therefore this response appeared and larvae were born, belonging to prodigies of shell-creatures.

sentences.zh: 景帝中三年秋，蝗。

translations.literal: In autumn of Emperor Jing, middle year three, there were locusts.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Emperor Jing, middle year three, there were locusts.

sentences.zh: 先是匈奴寇邊，中尉不害將車騎材官士屯代高柳。

translations.literal: Before this, the Xiongnu raided the border; commandant Bu Hai led chariot cavalry and trained soldiers to garrison Gaoliu in Dai.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, the Xiongnu raided the border; commandant Bu Hai led chariot cavalry and trained soldiers to garrison Gaoliu in Dai.

sentences.zh: 武帝元光五年秋，螟；

translations.literal: In autumn of Yuanguang year five under Emperor Wu: borers;
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Yuanguang year five under Emperor Wu: borers;

sentences.zh: 六年夏，蝗。

translations.literal: in summer of year six: locusts.
translations.idiomatic: in summer of year six: locusts.

sentences.zh: 先是，五將軍眾三十萬伏馬邑，欲襲單于也。

translations.literal: Before this, five generals with 300,000 troops lay in ambush at Mayi seeking to attack the Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, five generals with 300,000 troops lay in ambush at Mayi seeking to attack the Chanyu.

sentences.zh: 是歲，四將軍征匈奴。

translations.literal: That year, four generals campaigned against the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: That year, four generals campaigned against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 元鼎五年秋，蝗。

translations.literal: In autumn of Yuanding year five, locusts.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Yuanding year five, locusts.

sentences.zh: 是歲，四將軍征南越及西南夷，開十餘郡。

translations.literal: That year, four generals campaigned against Nanyue and the southwestern tribes, opening over ten commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: That year, four generals campaigned against Nanyue and the southwestern tribes, opening over ten commanderies.

sentences.zh: 元封六年秋，蝗。

translations.literal: In autumn of Yuanfeng year six, locusts.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Yuanfeng year six, locusts.

sentences.zh: 先是，兩將軍征朝鮮，開三郡。

translations.literal: Before this, two generals campaigned against Joseon and opened three commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, two generals campaigned against Joseon and opened three commanderies.

sentences.zh: 太初元年夏，蝗從東方蜚至敦煌；

translations.literal: In summer of Taichu year one, locusts flew from the east to Dunhuang;
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Taichu year one, locusts flew from the east to Dunhuang;

sentences.zh: 三年秋，復蝗。

translations.literal: in autumn of year three, locusts again.
translations.idiomatic: in autumn of year three, locusts again.

sentences.zh: 元年貳師將軍征大宛，天下奉其役連年。

translations.literal: In year one, General Ershi campaigned against Dayuan, and the realm supplied his labors year after year.
translations.idiomatic: In year one, General Ershi campaigned against Dayuan, and the realm supplied his labors year after year.

sentences.zh: 征和三年秋，蝗；

translations.literal: In autumn of Zhenghe year three, locusts;
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Zhenghe year three, locusts;

sentences.zh: 四年夏，蝗。

translations.literal: in summer of year four, locusts.
translations.idiomatic: in summer of year four, locusts.

sentences.zh: 先是一年，三將軍眾十餘萬征匈奴。

translations.literal: In the year before, three generals with over one hundred thousand troops campaigned against the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: In the year before, three generals with over one hundred thousand troops campaigned against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 征和三年，貳師七萬人沒不還。

translations.literal: In Zhenghe year three, Ershi's seventy thousand men were lost and did not return.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhenghe year three, Ershi's seventy thousand men were lost and did not return.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始二年秋，蝗，遍天下。

translations.literal: In autumn of Yuanshi year two under Emperor Ping, locusts spread throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Yuanshi year two under Emperor Ping, locusts spread throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 是時王莽秉政。

translations.literal: At that time Wang Mang held power.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Wang Mang held power.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰嚴公八年齊襄公田于貝丘，見豕。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says that in Duke Zhuang year eight, Duke Xiang of Qi hunted at Beiqiu and saw a boar.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says that in Duke Zhuang year eight, Duke Xiang of Qi hunted at Beiqiu and saw a boar.

sentences.zh: 從者曰：「公子彭生也。」

translations.literal: Attendants said, "It is Prince Pengsheng."
translations.idiomatic: Attendants said, "It is Prince Pengsheng."

sentences.zh: 公怒曰：「射之！」

translations.literal: The duke angrily said, "Shoot it!"
translations.idiomatic: The duke angrily said, "Shoot it!"

sentences.zh: 豕人立而啼，公懼，墜車，傷足喪屨。

translations.literal: The boar stood upright like a man and cried out; the duke was frightened, fell from his chariot, injured his foot, and lost a shoe.
translations.idiomatic: The boar stood upright like a man and cried out; the duke was frightened, fell from his chariot, injured his foot, and lost a shoe.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近豕禍也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near swine calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near swine calamity.

sentences.zh: 先是，齊襄淫於妹魯桓公夫人，使公子彭生殺威公，又殺彭生以謝魯。

translations.literal: Before this, Duke Xiang of Qi had illicit relations with his sister, wife of Duke Huan of Lu, made Prince Pengsheng kill Duke Huan, then killed Pengsheng to placate Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Duke Xiang of Qi had illicit relations with his sister, wife of Duke Huan of Lu, made Prince Pengsheng kill Duke Huan, then killed Pengsheng to placate Lu.

sentences.zh: 公孫無知有寵於先君，襄公絀之，無知帥怨恨之徒攻襄於田所，襄匿其戶間，足見於戶下，遂殺之。

translations.literal: Gongsun Wuzhi had been favored by former rulers, but Duke Xiang demoted him. Wuzhi led resentful followers to attack Xiang at the hunting place. Xiang hid between door panels, his foot visible below the door, and they killed him.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Wuzhi had been favored by former rulers, but Duke Xiang demoted him. Wuzhi led resentful followers to attack Xiang at the hunting place. Xiang hid between door panels, his foot visible below the door, and they killed him.

sentences.zh: 傷足喪屨，卒死於足，虐急之效也。

translations.literal: Injuring his foot and losing his shoe, then dying through his feet, was the effect of cruel urgency.
translations.idiomatic: Injuring his foot and losing his shoe, then dying through his feet, was the effect of cruel urgency.

sentences.zh: 史記曰，秦武王三年渭水赤者三日，昭王三十四年渭水又赤三日。

translations.literal: The Records says: in year three of King Wu of Qin, the Wei River ran red for three days; in year thirty-four of King Zhao, the Wei again ran red for three days.
translations.idiomatic: The Records says: in year three of King Wu of Qin, the Wei River ran red for three days; in year thirty-four of King Zhao, the Wei again ran red for three days.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近火沴水也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near case of fire afflicting water.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near case of fire afflicting water.

sentences.zh: 秦連相坐之法，棄灰於道者黥，罔密而刑虐，加以武伐橫出，殘賊鄰國，至於變亂五行，氣色謬亂。

translations.literal: Qin had a law of collective implication: even discarding ashes on the road brought tattooing. Nets were dense and punishments cruel, plus martial campaigns launched arbitrarily, ravaging neighboring states, until the five phases were thrown into disorder and their qi-colors chaotic.
translations.idiomatic: Qin had a law of collective implication: even discarding ashes on the road brought tattooing. Nets were dense and punishments cruel, plus martial campaigns launched arbitrarily, ravaging neighboring states, until the five phases were thrown into disorder and their qi-colors chaotic.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，勿為刻急，將致敗亡。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not be harsh and severe, or you will bring ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not be harsh and severe, or you will bring ruin.

sentences.zh: 秦遂不改，至始皇滅六國，二世而亡。

translations.literal: Qin never changed; First Emperor eventually destroyed the six states, and by the second generation Qin perished.
translations.idiomatic: Qin never changed; First Emperor eventually destroyed the six states, and by the second generation Qin perished.

sentences.zh: 昔三代居三河，河洛出圖書，秦居渭陽，而渭水數赤，瑞異應德之效也。

translations.literal: Formerly the Three Dynasties dwelt by the three rivers, and the Yellow and Luo produced charts and writings; Qin dwelt at Weiyang, and the Wei repeatedly ran red. Auspicious and anomalous responses correspond to virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly the Three Dynasties dwelt by the three rivers, and the Yellow and Luo produced charts and writings; Qin dwelt at Weiyang, and the Wei repeatedly ran red. Auspicious and anomalous responses correspond to virtue.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「君湎于酒，淫于色，賢人潛，國家危，厥異流水赤也。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the ruler is soaked in wine and licentious in lust, worthy men hide and the state is endangered, its anomaly is flowing waters turning red."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the ruler is soaked in wine and licentious in lust, worthy men hide and the state is endangered, its anomaly is flowing waters turning red."
